     4	  module Protocols
     5	    class ChatCompletions
     6	      # Chat methods of the OpenAI API integration
     7	      module Chat
     8	        def completion_url
     9	          'chat/completions'
    10	        end
    11	
    12	        module_function
    13	
    14	        # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists,Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
    15	        def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false, schema: nil,
    16	                           thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
    17	          warn_unsupported_citations(model) if citations && !model.citations?
    18	          tool_prefs ||= {}
    19	          payload = {
    20	            model: model.id,
    21	            messages: format_messages(messages),
    22	            stream: stream
    23	          }
    24	
    25	          payload[:temperature] = temperature unless temperature.nil?
    26	          if tools.any?
    27	            payload[:tools] = tools.map { |_, tool| tool_for(tool) }
    28	            payload[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_prefs[:choice]) unless tool_prefs[:choice].nil?
    29	            payload[:parallel_tool_calls] = tool_prefs[:calls] == :many unless tool_prefs[:calls].nil?
    30	          end
    31	
    32	          if schema
    33	            schema_name = schema[:name]
    34	            schema_def = schema[:schema]
    35	            strict = schema[:strict]
    36	
    37	            payload[:response_format] = {
    38	              type: 'json_schema',
    39	              json_schema: {
    40	                name: schema_name,
    41	                schema: schema_def,
    42	                strict: strict
    43	              }
    44	            }
    45	          end
    46	
    47	          effort = resolve_effort(thinking)
    48	          payload[:reasoning_effort] = effort if effort
    49	
    50	          payload[:stream_options] = { include_usage: true } if stream
    51	          payload
    52	        end
    53	        # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists,Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
    54	
    55	        def warn_unsupported_citations(model)
    56	          RubyLLM.logger.warn(
    57	            "#{model.id} does not support citations according to the model registry. " \
    58	            'with_citations may have no effect.'
    59	          )
    60	        end
    61	
    62	        def parse_completion_response(response)
    63	          data = response.body
    64	          return if data.nil? || data.empty?
    65	
    66	          raise Error.new(response, data.dig('error', 'message')) if data.dig('error', 'message')
    67	
    68	          message_data = data.dig('choices', 0, 'message')
    69	          return unless message_data
    70	
    71	          usage = data['usage'] || {}
    72	          thinking_tokens = thinking_tokens(usage)
    73	          content, thinking_from_blocks = extract_content_and_thinking(message_data['content'])
    74	          thinking_text = thinking_from_blocks || extract_thinking_text(message_data)
    75	          thinking_signature = extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
    76	
    77	          Message.new(
    78	            role: :assistant,
    79	            content: content,
    80	            citations: extract_citations(message_data, data, content),
    81	            thinking: Thinking.build(text: thinking_text, signature: thinking_signature),
    82	            tool_calls: parse_tool_calls(message_data['tool_calls']),
    83	            input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
    84	            output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
    85	            cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
    86	            cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),
    87	            thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens,
    88	            model_id: data['model'],
    89	            raw: response
    90	          )
    91	        end
    92	
    93	        def input_tokens(usage)
    94	          return usage['prompt_cache_miss_tokens'] if usage['prompt_cache_miss_tokens']
    95	
    96	          prompt_tokens = usage['prompt_tokens']
    97	          return unless prompt_tokens
    98	
    99	          [prompt_tokens.to_i - cache_read_tokens(usage).to_i - cache_write_tokens(usage).to_i, 0].max
   100	        end
   101	
   102	        def output_tokens(usage)
   103	          completion_tokens = usage['completion_tokens']
   104	          return unless completion_tokens
   105	
   106	          completion_tokens = completion_tokens.to_i
   107	          generated_tokens = generated_tokens_from_total(usage)
   108	          return completion_tokens unless generated_tokens && generated_tokens > completion_tokens
   109	
   110	          generated_tokens
   111	        end
   112	
   113	        def generated_tokens_from_total(usage)
   114	          prompt_tokens = usage['prompt_tokens']
   115	          total_tokens = usage['total_tokens']
   116	          return unless prompt_tokens && total_tokens
   117	
   118	          [total_tokens.to_i - prompt_tokens.to_i, 0].max
   119	        end
   120	
   121	        def cache_read_tokens(usage)
   122	          usage.dig('prompt_tokens_details', 'cached_tokens') || usage['prompt_cache_hit_tokens']
   123	        end
   124	
   125	        def cache_write_tokens(usage)
   126	          usage.dig('prompt_tokens_details', 'cache_write_tokens') || 0
   127	        end
   128	
   129	        def thinking_tokens(usage)
   130	          usage.dig('completion_tokens_details', 'reasoning_tokens') || usage['reasoning_tokens']
   131	        end
   132	
   133	        def extract_citations(message_data, data, content)
   134	          annotations = parse_annotations(message_data['annotations'], content)
   135	          return annotations if annotations.any?
   136	
   137	          parse_root_citations(data)
   138	        end
   139	
   140	        def parse_annotations(annotations, content)
   141	          Array(annotations).filter_map do |annotation|
   142	            details = annotation['url_citation']
   143	            next unless details.is_a?(Hash)
   144	
   145	            start_index = details['start_index']
   146	            end_index = details['end_index']
   147	
   148	            Citation.new(
   149	              url: details['url'],
   150	              title: details['title'],
   151	              text: annotated_text(content, start_index, end_index),
   152	              start_index: start_index,
   153	              end_index: end_index
   154	            )
   155	          end
   156	        end
   157	
   158	        def annotated_text(content, start_index, end_index)
   159	          return nil unless content.is_a?(String) && start_index && end_index
   160	
   161	          content[start_index...end_index]
   162	        end
   163	
   164	        # Perplexity and xAI return search citations at the root of the response.
   165	        def parse_root_citations(data)
   166	          search_results = data['search_results']
   167	          return parse_search_results(search_results) if search_results.is_a?(Array) && search_results.any?
   168	
   169	          Array(data['citations']).each_with_index.filter_map do |url, index|
   170	            Citation.new(url: url, source_index: index) if url.is_a?(String)
   171	          end
   172	        end
   173	
   174	        def parse_search_results(results)
   175	          results.each_with_index.filter_map do |result, index|
   176	            next unless result.is_a?(Hash)
   177	
   178	            Citation.new(
   179	              url: result['url'],
   180	              title: result['title'],
   181	              cited_text: result['snippet'],
   182	              source_index: index
   183	            )
   184	          end
   185	        end
   186	
   187	        def format_messages(messages)
   188	          messages.map do |msg|
   189	            {
   190	              role: format_role(msg.role),
   191	              content: format_message_content(msg),
   192	              tool_calls: format_tool_calls(msg.tool_calls),
   193	              tool_call_id: msg.tool_call_id
   194	            }.compact.merge(format_thinking(msg))
   195	          end
   196	        end
   197	
   198	        def format_message_content(msg)
   199	          content = format_content(msg.content)
   200	          return '' if content.nil? && thinking_only_assistant_message?(msg)
   201	
   202	          content
   203	        end
   204	
   205	        def thinking_only_assistant_message?(msg)
   206	          msg.role == :assistant && msg.thinking && !msg.tool_call?
   207	        end
   208	
   209	        def format_content(content)
   210	          Media.format_content(content)
   211	        end
   212	
   213	        def format_role(role)
   214	          case role
   215	          when :system
   216	            @config.openai_use_system_role ? 'system' : 'developer'
   217	          else
   218	            role.to_s
   219	          end
   220	        end
   221	
   222	        def resolve_effort(thinking)
   223	          return nil unless thinking
   224	
   225	          thinking.respond_to?(:effort) ? thinking.effort : thinking
   226	        end
   227	
   228	        def format_thinking(msg)
   229	          return {} unless msg.role == :assistant
   230	
   231	          thinking = msg.thinking
   232	          return {} unless thinking
   233	
   234	          payload = {}
   235	          if thinking.text
   236	            payload[:reasoning] = thinking.text
   237	            payload[:reasoning_content] = thinking.text
   238	          end
   239	          payload[:reasoning_signature] = thinking.signature if thinking.signature
   240	          payload
   241	        end
   242	
   243	        def extract_thinking_text(message_data)
   244	          candidate = message_data['reasoning_content'] || message_data['reasoning'] || message_data['thinking']
   245	          candidate.is_a?(String) ? candidate : nil
   246	        end
   247	
   248	        def extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
   249	          candidate = message_data['reasoning_signature'] || message_data['signature']
   250	          candidate.is_a?(String) ? candidate : nil
   251	        end
   252	
   253	        def extract_content_and_thinking(content)
   254	          return extract_think_tag_content(content) if content.is_a?(String)
   255	          return [content, nil] unless content.is_a?(Array)
   256	
   257	          text = extract_text_from_blocks(content)
   258	          thinking = extract_thinking_from_blocks(content)
   259	
   260	          [text.empty? ? nil : text, thinking.empty? ? nil : thinking]
   261	        end
   262	
   263	        def extract_text_from_blocks(blocks)
   264	          blocks.filter_map do |block|
   265	            block['text'] if block['type'] == 'text' && block['text'].is_a?(String)
   266	          end.join
   267	        end
   268	
   269	        def extract_thinking_from_blocks(blocks)
   270	          blocks.filter_map do |block|
   271	            next unless block['type'] == 'thinking'
   272	
   273	            extract_thinking_text_from_block(block)
   274	          end.join
   275	        end
   276	
   277	        def extract_thinking_text_from_block(block)
   278	          thinking_block = block['thinking']
   279	          return thinking_block if thinking_block.is_a?(String)
   280	
   281	          if thinking_block.is_a?(Array)
   282	            return thinking_block.filter_map { |item| item['text'] if item['type'] == 'text' }.join
   283	          end
   284	
   285	          block['text'] if block['text'].is_a?(String)
   286	        end
   287	
   288	        def extract_think_tag_content(text)
   289	          return [text, nil] unless text.include?('<think>')
   290	
   291	          thinking = text.scan(%r{<think>(.*?)</think>}m).join
   292	          content = text.gsub(%r{<think>.*?</think>}m, '').strip
   293	
   294	          [content.empty? ? nil : content, thinking.empty? ? nil : thinking]
   295	        end
   296	      end
   297	    end
   298	  end
   299	end

    25	              capabilities: capabilities.critical_capabilities_for(model_id),
    26	              pricing: capabilities.pricing_for(model_id),
    27	              metadata: {

    30	              }

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  module Providers
     5	    # OpenRouter API integration.
     6	    class OpenRouter < Provider
     7	      # OpenRouter's dialect of the Chat Completions API.
     8	      class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions
     9	        include OpenRouter::Chat
    10	        include OpenRouter::Images
    11	        include OpenRouter::Models
    12	        include OpenRouter::Streaming
    13	      end
    14	
    15	      protocol :chat_completions, ChatCompletions
    16	
    17	      def api_base
    18	        @config.openrouter_api_base || 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'
    19	      end
    20	
    21	      def headers
    22	        {
    23	          'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.openrouter_api_key}"
    24	        }
    25	      end
    26	
    27	      def parse_error(response)
    28	        return if response.body.empty?
    29	
    30	        body = try_parse_json(response.body)
    31	        case body
    32	        when Hash
    33	          parse_error_part_message body
    34	        when Array
    35	          body.map do |part|
    36	            parse_error_part_message part
    37	          end.join('. ')
    38	        else
    39	          body
    40	        end
    41	      end
    42	
    43	      class << self
    44	        def configuration_options
    45	          %i[openrouter_api_key openrouter_api_base]
    46	        end
    47	
    48	        def configuration_requirements
    49	          %i[openrouter_api_key]
    50	        end
    51	      end
    52	
    53	      private
    54	
    55	      def parse_error_part_message(part)
    56	        message = part.dig('error', 'message')
    57	        raw = try_parse_json(part.dig('error', 'metadata', 'raw'))
    58	        return message unless raw.is_a?(Hash)
    59	
    60	        raw_message = raw.dig('error', 'message')
    61	        return [message, raw_message].compact.join(' - ') if raw_message
    62	
    63	        message
    64	      end
    65	    end
    66	  end
    67	end

     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  module Providers
     5	    # Perplexity API integration.
     6	    class Perplexity < Provider
     7	      # Perplexity's dialect of the Chat Completions API.
     8	      class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions
     9	        include Perplexity::Chat
    10	        include Perplexity::Models
    11	      end
    12	
    13	      protocol :chat_completions, ChatCompletions
    14	
    15	      def api_base
    16	        @config.perplexity_api_base || 'https://api.perplexity.ai'
    17	      end
    18	
    19	      def headers
    20	        {
    21	          'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.perplexity_api_key}",
    22	          'Content-Type' => 'application/json'
    23	        }
    24	      end
    25	
    26	      def parse_error(response)
    27	        body = response.body
    28	        return if body.empty?
    29	
    30	        # If response is HTML (Perplexity returns HTML for auth errors)
    31	        if body.include?('<html>') && body.include?('<title>')
    32	          title_match = body.match(%r{<title>(.+?)</title>})
    33	          if title_match
    34	            message = title_match[1]
    35	            message = message.sub(/^\d+\s+/, '')
    36	            return message
    37	          end
    38	        end
    39	        super
    40	      end

    43	        def capabilities
    44	          Perplexity::Capabilities
    45	        end

    49	        end
    50

    52	          %i[perplexity_api_key]
    53	        end
    54	      end
    55	    end
    56	  end
    57	end

     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  module Providers
     5	    # Azure AI Foundry / OpenAI-compatible API integration.
     6	    class Azure < Provider
     7	      protocol :chat_completions, Azure::ChatCompletions
     8	
     9	      def api_base
    10	        @config.azure_api_base
    11	      end
    12	
    13	      def headers
    14	        if @config.azure_api_key
    15	          { 'api-key' => @config.azure_api_key }
    16	        else
    17	          { 'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.azure_ai_auth_token}" }
    18	        end
    19	      end
    20	
    21	      class << self
    22	        def configuration_options
    23	          %i[azure_api_base azure_api_key azure_ai_auth_token]
    24	        end
    25	
    26	        def configuration_requirements
    27	          %i[azure_api_base]
    28	        end
    29	
    30	        def configured?(config)
    31	          config.azure_api_base && (config.azure_api_key || config.azure_ai_auth_token)
    32	        end
    33	
    34	        # Azure works with deployment names, instead of model names
    35	        def assume_models_exist?
    36	          true
    37	        end
    38	      end
    39	
    40	      def ensure_configured!
    41	        missing = []
    42	        missing << :azure_api_base unless @config.azure_api_base
    43	        if @config.azure_api_key.nil? && @config.azure_ai_auth_token.nil?
    44	          missing << 'azure_api_key or azure_ai_auth_token'
    45	        end
    46	        return if missing.empty?
    47	
    48	        raise ConfigurationError,
    49	              "Missing configuration for Azure: #{missing.join(', ')}"
    50	      end
    51	    end
    52	  end
    53	end

     4	  module Providers

     6	      # Chat methods of the DeepSeek API integration
     7	      module Chat
     8	        module_function
     9	
    10	        def format_role(role)
    11	          role.to_s
    12	        end
    13	
    14	        def format_content(content)
    15	          Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_content(
    16	            content,
    17	            document_attachments: :none,
    18	            image_attachments: false,

FILE:lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/chat.rb
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  module Providers
     5	    class Mistral
     6	      # Chat methods for Mistral API
     7	      module Chat
     8	        module_function
     9	
    10	        def format_role(role)
    11	          role.to_s
    12	        end
    13	
    14	        def format_messages(messages)
    15	          messages.map do |msg|
    16	            {
    17	              role: format_role(msg.role),
    18	              content: format_content_with_thinking(msg),
    19	              tool_calls: Protocols::ChatCompletions::Tools.format_tool_calls(msg.tool_calls),
    20	              tool_call_id: msg.tool_call_id
    21	            }.compact
    22	          end
    23	        end
    24	
    25	        # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
    26	        def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false,
    27	                           schema: nil, thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
    28	          payload = super
    29	          payload.delete(:stream_options)
    30	          configure_thinking_payload(payload, model, thinking)
    31	          normalize_required_tool_choice(payload)
    32	          payload
    33	        end
    34	        # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
    35	
    36	        def build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
    37	          return 'any' if tool_choice == :required
    38	
    39	          Protocols::ChatCompletions::Tools.build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
    40	        end
    41	
    42	        def normalize_required_tool_choice(payload)
    43	          return unless payload[:tool_choice] == 'any' && Array(payload[:tools]).one?
    44	
    45	          function_name = payload.dig(:tools, 0, :function, :name)
    46	          return unless function_name
    47	
    48	          payload[:tool_choice] = {
    49	            type: 'function',
    50	            function: { name: function_name }
    51	          }
    52	        end
    53	
    54	        def format_content_with_thinking(msg)
    55	          formatted_content = Mistral::Media.format_content(msg.content)
    56	          return formatted_content unless msg.role == :assistant && msg.thinking
    57	
    58	          content_blocks = build_thinking_blocks(msg.thinking)
    59	          append_formatted_content(content_blocks, formatted_content)
    60	
    61	          content_blocks
    62	        end
    63	
    64	        def warn_on_unsupported_thinking(model, thinking)
    65	          return unless thinking&.enabled?
    66	          return if native_reasoning_model?(model.id) || adjustable_reasoning_model?(model.id)
    67	
    68	          RubyLLM.logger.warn(
    69	            'Mistral thinking is only supported on Magistral and adjustable-reasoning models. ' \
    70	            "Ignoring thinking settings for #{model.id}."
    71	          )
    72	        end
    73	
    74	        def configure_thinking_payload(payload, model, thinking)
    75	          return unless thinking&.enabled?
    76	
    77	          if native_reasoning_model?(model.id)
    78	            configure_native_reasoning_payload(payload, thinking)
    79	          elsif adjustable_reasoning_model?(model.id)
    80	            payload[:reasoning_effort] = reasoning_effort_for(thinking)
    81	          else
    82	            payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
    83	            warn_on_unsupported_thinking(model, thinking)
    84	          end
    85	        end
    86	
    87	        def configure_native_reasoning_payload(payload, thinking)
    88	          payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
    89	          payload[:prompt_mode] = thinking.effort == 'none' ? nil : 'reasoning'
    90	        end
    91	
    92	        def reasoning_effort_for(thinking)
    93	          effort = thinking.respond_to?(:effort) ? thinking.effort : nil
    94	          return effort if %w[high none].include?(effort)
    95	
    96	          'high'
    97	        end
    98	
    99	        def native_reasoning_model?(model_id)
   100	          model_id.to_s.include?('magistral')
   101	        end
   102	
   103	        def adjustable_reasoning_model?(model_id)
   104	          model_id.to_s.match?(/\Amistral-(?:small-latest|medium-(?:3(?:[.-]5)?|latest))\z/)
   105	        end
   106	
   107	        def build_thinking_blocks(thinking)
   108	          return [] unless thinking
   109	
   110	          if thinking.text
   111	            [{
   112	              type: 'thinking',
   113	              thinking: [{ type: 'text', text: thinking.text }],
   114	              signature: thinking.signature
   115	            }.compact]
   116	          elsif thinking.signature
   117	            [{ type: 'thinking', signature: thinking.signature }]
   118	          else
   119	            []
   120	          end
   121	        end
   122	
   123	        def append_formatted_content(content_blocks, formatted_content)
   124	          if formatted_content.is_a?(Array)
   125	            content_blocks.concat(formatted_content)
   126	          elsif formatted_content
   127	            content_blocks << { type: 'text', text: formatted_content }
   128	          end
   129	        end
   130	      end
   131	    end
   132	  end
   133	end

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  module Providers
     5	    class OpenRouter
     6	      # Chat methods of the OpenRouter API integration
     7	      module Chat
     8	        module_function
     9	
    10	        # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
    11	        def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false, schema: nil,
    12	                           thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
    13	          payload = super
    14	          payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
    15	          strip_schema_strict(payload)
    16	
    17	          reasoning = build_reasoning(thinking)
    18	          payload[:reasoning] = reasoning if reasoning
    19	          payload
    20	        end
    21	        # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
    22	
    23	        def strip_schema_strict(payload)
    24	          schema_def = payload.dig(:response_format, :json_schema, :schema)
    25	          return unless schema_def.is_a?(Hash)
    26	
    27	          schema_def = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_dup(schema_def)
    28	          schema_def.delete(:strict)
    29	          schema_def.delete('strict')
    30	          payload[:response_format][:json_schema][:schema] = schema_def
    31	        end
    32	
    33	        def build_reasoning(thinking)
    34	          return nil unless thinking&.enabled?
    35	
    36	          reasoning = {}
    37	          reasoning[:effort] = thinking.effort if thinking.respond_to?(:effort) && thinking.effort
    38	          reasoning[:max_tokens] = thinking.budget if thinking.respond_to?(:budget) && thinking.budget
    39	          reasoning[:enabled] = true if reasoning.empty?
    40	          reasoning
    41	        end
    42	
    43	        def format_thinking(msg)
    44	          thinking = msg.thinking
    45	          return {} unless thinking && msg.role == :assistant
    46	
    47	          details = []
    48	          if thinking.text
    49	            details << {
    50	              type: 'reasoning.text',
    51	              text: thinking.text,
    52	              signature: thinking.signature
    53	            }.compact
    54	          elsif thinking.signature
    55	            details << {
    56	              type: 'reasoning.encrypted',
    57	              data: thinking.signature
    58	            }
    59	          end
    60	
    61	          details.empty? ? {} : { reasoning_details: details }
    62	        end
    63	
    64	        def extract_thinking_text(message_data)
    65	          candidate = message_data['reasoning']
    66	          return candidate if candidate.is_a?(String)
    67	
    68	          details = message_data['reasoning_details']
    69	          return nil unless details.is_a?(Array)
    70	
    71	          text = details.filter_map do |detail|
    72	            case detail['type']
    73	            when 'reasoning.text'
    74	              detail['text']
    75	            when 'reasoning.summary'
    76	              detail['summary']
    77	            end
    78	          end.join
    79	
    80	          text.empty? ? nil : text
    81	        end
    82	
    83	        def extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
    84	          details = message_data['reasoning_details']
    85	          return nil unless details.is_a?(Array)
    86	
    87	          signature = details.filter_map do |detail|
    88	            detail['signature'] if detail['signature'].is_a?(String)
    89	          end.first
    90	          return signature if signature
    91	
    92	          encrypted = details.find { |detail| detail['type'] == 'reasoning.encrypted' && detail['data'].is_a?(String) }
    93	          encrypted&.dig('data')
    94	        end
    95	      end
    96	    end
    97	  end
    98	end

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  module Providers
     5	    class OpenRouter
     6	      # Image generation methods for the OpenRouter API integration.
     7	      # OpenRouter uses the chat completions endpoint for image generation
     8	      # instead of a dedicated images endpoint.
     9	      module Images
    10	        module_function
    11	
    12	        def images_url(with: nil, mask: nil) # rubocop:disable Lint/UnusedMethodArgument
    13	          'chat/completions'
    14	        end
    15	
    16	        def render_image_payload(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Lint/UnusedMethodArgument,Metrics/ParameterLists
    17	          RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Ignoring size #{size}. OpenRouter image generation does not support size parameter." }
    18	          {
    19	            model: model,
    20	            messages: [
    21	              {
    22	                role: 'user',
    23	                content: prompt
    24	              }
    25	            ],
    26	            modalities: %w[image text]
    27	          }
    28	        end
    29	
    30	        def parse_image_response(response, model:)
    31	          data = response.body
    32	          message = data.dig('choices', 0, 'message')
    33	
    34	          unless message&.key?('images') && message['images']&.any?
    35	            raise Error.new(nil, 'Unexpected response format from OpenRouter image generation API')
    36	          end
    37	
    38	          image_data = message['images'].first
    39	          image_url = image_data.dig('image_url', 'url') || image_data['url']
    40	
    41	          raise Error.new(nil, 'No image URL found in OpenRouter response') unless image_url
    42	
    43	          build_image_from_url(image_url, model)
    44	        end
    45	
    46	        def build_image_from_url(image_url, model)
    47	          if image_url.start_with?('data:')
    48	            # Parse data URL format: data:image/png;base64,<data>
    49	            match = image_url.match(/^data:([^;]+);base64,(.+)$/)
    50	            raise Error.new(nil, 'Invalid data URL format from OpenRouter') unless match
    51	
    52	            Image.new(
    53	              data: match[2],
    54	              mime_type: match[1],
    55	              model_id: model
    56	            )
    57	          else
    58	            # Regular URL
    59	            Image.new(
    60	              url: image_url,
    61	              mime_type: 'image/png',
    62	              model_id: model
    63	            )
    64	          end
    65	        end
    66	      end
    67	    end
    68	  end
    69	end

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  module Providers
     5	    class OpenRouter
     6	      # Models methods of the OpenRouter API integration
     7	      module Models
     8	        module_function
     9	
    10	        def models_url
    11	          'models'
    12	        end
    13	
    14	        def parse_list_models_response(response, slug, _capabilities)
    15	          Array(response.body['data']).map do |model_data| # rubocop:disable Metrics/BlockLength
    16	            modalities = {
    17	              input: Array(model_data.dig('architecture', 'input_modalities')),
    18	              output: Array(model_data.dig('architecture', 'output_modalities'))
    19	            }
    20	
    21	            pricing = { text_tokens: { standard: {} } }
    22	
    23	            pricing_types = {
    24	              prompt: :input_per_million,
    25	              completion: :output_per_million,
    26	              input_cache_read: :cache_read_input_per_million,
    27	              internal_reasoning: :reasoning_output_per_million
    28	            }
    29	
    30	            pricing_types.each do |source_key, target_key|
    31	              value = model_data.dig('pricing', source_key.to_s).to_f
    32	              pricing[:text_tokens][:standard][target_key] = value * 1_000_000 if value.positive?
    33	            end
    34	
    35	            capabilities = supported_parameters_to_capabilities(model_data['supported_parameters'])
    36	
    37	            Model::Info.new(
    38	              id: model_data['id'],
    39	              name: model_data['name'],
    40	              provider: slug,
    41	              family: model_data['id'].split('/').first,
    42	              created_at: model_data['created'] ? Time.at(model_data['created']) : nil,
    43	              context_window: model_data['context_length'],
    44	              max_output_tokens: model_data.dig('top_provider', 'max_completion_tokens'),
    45	              modalities: modalities,
    46	              capabilities: capabilities,
    47	              pricing: pricing,
    48	              metadata: {
    49	                description: model_data['description'],
    50	                architecture: model_data['architecture'],
    51	                top_provider: model_data['top_provider'],
    52	                per_request_limits: model_data['per_request_limits'],
    53	                supported_parameters: model_data['supported_parameters']
    54	              }
    55	            )
    56	          end
    57	        end
    58	
    59	        def supported_parameters_to_capabilities(params)
    60	          return [] unless params
    61	
    62	          capabilities = []
    63	          capabilities << 'streaming'
    64	          capabilities << 'function_calling' if params.include?('tools') || params.include?('tool_choice')
    65	          capabilities << 'structured_output' if params.include?('response_format')
    66	          capabilities << 'batch' if params.include?('batch')
    67	          capabilities << 'predicted_outputs' if params.include?('logit_bias') && params.include?('top_k')
    68	          capabilities
    69	        end
    70	      end
    71	    end
    72	  end
    73	end

    12	          delta = data.dig('choices', 0, 'delta') || {}
    13	
    14	          Chunk.new(
    15	            role: :assistant,

    23	            input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
    24	            output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
    25	            cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
    26	            cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),

     5	    class Perplexity
     6	      # Handles Perplexity Sonar media content.
     7	      module Media
     8	        module_function
     9	
    10	        SUPPORTED_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS = %w[pdf doc docx txt rtf].freeze
    11	
    12	        def format_content(content)
    13	          Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_parts(content) do |attachment|
    14	            case attachment.type
    15	            when :image
    16	              Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_image(attachment)
    17	            when :pdf, :document
    18	              format_document(attachment)
    19	            when :text
    20	              Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_text_file(attachment)
    21	            else
    22	              raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
    23	            end
    24	          end
    25	        end
    26	
    27	        def format_document(attachment)
    28	          raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless supported_file?(attachment)
    29	
    30	          {
    31	            type: 'file_url',
    32	            file_url: {
    33	              url: attachment.url? ? attachment.source.to_s : attachment.encoded
    34	            }
    35	          }
    36	        end
    37	
    38	        def supported_file?(attachment)
    39	          return true if attachment.pdf?
    40	
    41	          SUPPORTED_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS.include?(attachment.extension)
    42	        end
    43	      end
    44	    end
    45	  end
    46	end

     3	require 'time'
     4	
     5	module RubyLLM
     6	  module Providers
     7	    class GPUStack
     8	      # Models methods of the GPUStack API integration
     9	      module Models
    10	        module_function
    11	
    12	        def models_url
    13	          'models'
    14	        end
    15	
    16	        def parse_list_models_response(response, slug, _capabilities)
    17	          items = response.body['items'] || []
    18	          items.map do |model|
    19	            Model::Info.new(
    20	              id: model['name'],
    21	              name: model['name'],
    22	              created_at: model['created_at'] ? Time.parse(model['created_at']) : nil,
    23	              provider: slug,
    24	              family: 'gpustack',
    25	              metadata: {
    26	                description: model['description'],
    27	                source: model['source'],
    28	                huggingface_repo_id: model['huggingface_repo_id'],
    29	                ollama_library_model_name: model['ollama_library_model_name'],
    30	                backend: model['backend'],
    31	                meta: model['meta'],
    32	                categories: model['categories']
    33	              },
    34	              context_window: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
    35	              max_output_tokens: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
    36	              capabilities: build_capabilities(model),
    37	              modalities: build_modalities(model),
    38	              pricing: {}
    39	            )
    40	          end
    41	        end
    42	
    43	        private
    44	
    45	        def build_capabilities(model) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
    46	          capabilities = []
    47	
    48	          # Add streaming by default for LLM models
    49	          capabilities << 'streaming' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
    50	
    51	          # Map GPUStack metadata to standard capabilities
    52	          capabilities << 'function_calling' if model.dig('meta', 'support_tool_calls')
    53	          capabilities << 'vision' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
    54	          capabilities << 'reasoning' if model.dig('meta', 'support_reasoning')
    55	
    56	          # GPUStack models generally support structured output and json mode
    57	          capabilities << 'structured_output' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
    58	          capabilities << 'json_mode' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
    59	
    60	          capabilities
    61	        end
    62	
    63	        def build_modalities(model)
    64	          input_modalities = []
    65	          output_modalities = []
    66	
    67	          if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
    68	            input_modalities << 'text'
    69	            input_modalities << 'image' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
    70	            input_modalities << 'audio' if model.dig('meta', 'support_audio')

    74	            output_modalities << 'embeddings'

    10	        def parse_list_models_response(response, slug, _capabilities)
    11	          Array(response.body['data']).map do |model_data|
    12	            model_id = model_data['id']
    13	
    14	            Model::Info.new(
    15	              id: model_id,
    16	              name: format_display_name(model_id),
    17	              provider: slug,
    18	              family: 'grok',
    19	              created_at: model_data['created'] ? Time.at(model_data['created']) : nil,
    20	              context_window: nil,
    21	              max_output_tokens: nil,
    22	              modalities: modalities_for(model_id),
    23	              capabilities: capabilities_for(model_id),
    24	              pricing: {},
    25	              metadata: {
    26	                object: model_data['object'],
    27	                owned_by: model_data['owned_by']
    28	              }.compact
    29	            )
    30	          end
    31	        end
    32	
    33	        def modalities_for(model_id)
    34	          if image_model?(model_id)
    35	            { input: %w[text image], output: ['image'] }
    36	          elsif video_model?(model_id)
    37	            { input: %w[text image video], output: ['video'] }
    38	          else
    39	            { input: ['text'], output: ['text'] }
    40	          end
    41	        end
    42	
    43	        def capabilities_for(model_id)
    44	          return ['vision'] if image_model?(model_id) || video_model?(model_id)
    45	
    46	          ['streaming']
    47	        end
    48	
    49	        def format_display_name(model_id)
    50	          model_id.tr('-', ' ').split.map(&:capitalize).join(' ')
    51	        end

    53	        def image_model?(model_id)
    54	          model_id.match?(/\Agrok-(?:2-)?imagine-image/) || model_id == 'grok-2-image-1212'
    55	        end
    56	
    57	        def video_model?(model_id)

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  module Providers
     5	    class Azure < Provider
     6	      # Azure's dialect of the Chat Completions API. Endpoints depend on how
     7	      # the configured api_base is shaped (deployment, resource, or openai/v1).
     8	      class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions
     9	        AZURE_DEFAULT_CHAT_API_VERSION = '2024-05-01-preview'
    10	        AZURE_DEFAULT_MODELS_API_VERSION = 'preview'
    11	
    12	        include Azure::Chat
    13	        include Azure::Embeddings
    14	        include Azure::Media
    15	        include Azure::Models
    16	
    17	        def azure_endpoint(kind)
    18	          parts = azure_base_parts
    19	
    20	          case kind
    21	          when :chat
    22	            chat_endpoint(parts)
    23	          when :embeddings
    24	            embeddings_endpoint(parts)
    25	          when :models
    26	            models_endpoint(parts)
    27	          else
    28	            raise ArgumentError, "Unknown Azure endpoint kind: #{kind.inspect}"
    29	          end
    30	        end
    31	
    32	        private
    33	
    34	        def azure_base_parts
    35	          @azure_base_parts ||= begin
    36	            raw_base = @provider.api_base.to_s.sub(%r{/+\z}, '')
    37	            version = raw_base[/[?&]api-version=([^&]+)/i, 1]
    38	            path_base = raw_base.sub(/\?.*\z/, '')
    39	
    40	            mode = if path_base.include?('/chat/completions')
    41	                     :chat_endpoint
    42	                   elsif path_base.include?('/openai/deployments/')
    43	                     :deployment_base
    44	                   elsif path_base.include?('/openai/v1')
    45	                     :openai_v1_base
    46	                   else
    47	                     :resource_base
    48	                   end
    49	
    50	            {
    51	              raw_base: raw_base,
    52	              path_base: path_base,
    53	              root: azure_host_root(path_base),
    54	              mode: mode,
    55	              version: version
    56	            }
    57	          end
    58	        end
    59	
    60	        def chat_endpoint(parts)
    61	          case parts[:mode]
    62	          when :chat_endpoint
    63	            ''
    64	          when :deployment_base
    65	            with_api_version('chat/completions', parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_CHAT_API_VERSION)
    66	          when :openai_v1_base
    67	            with_api_version('chat/completions', parts[:version])
    68	          else
    69	            with_api_version('models/chat/completions', parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_CHAT_API_VERSION)
    70	          end
    71	        end
    72	
    73	        def embeddings_endpoint(parts)
    74	          case parts[:mode]
    75	          when :deployment_base, :openai_v1_base
    76	            with_api_version('embeddings', parts[:version])
    77	          else
    78	            "#{parts[:root]}/openai/v1/embeddings"
    79	          end
    80	        end
    81	
    82	        def models_endpoint(parts)
    83	          case parts[:mode]
    84	          when :openai_v1_base
    85	            with_api_version('models', parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_MODELS_API_VERSION)
    86	          else
    87	            "#{parts[:root]}/openai/v1/models?api-version=#{parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_MODELS_API_VERSION}"
    88	          end
    89	        end
    90	
    91	        def with_api_version(path, version)
    92	          return path unless version
    93	
    94	          separator = path.include?('?') ? '&' : '?'
    95	          "#{path}#{separator}api-version=#{version}"
    96	        end
    97	
    98	        def azure_host_root(base_without_query)
    99	          base_without_query.sub(%r{/(models|openai)/.*\z}, '').sub(%r{/+\z}, '')
   100	        end
   101	      end
   102	    end
   103	  end
   104	end

     6	      # Handles formatting of media content (images, audio) for Azure OpenAI-compatible APIs.
     7	      module Media
     8	        module_function

    10	        def format_content(content)
    11	          Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_parts(content) do |attachment|
    12	            case attachment.type
    13	            when :image
    14	              format_image(attachment)
    15	            when :audio

    17	            when :text

    26	          {
    27	            type: 'image_url',
    28	            image_url: {
    29	              url: image.for_llm
    30	            }
    31	          }
    32	        end
    33	      end
    34	    end
    35	  end

     3	require 'faraday'
     4	require 'faraday/multipart'
     5	require 'faraday/retry'
     6	require 'ruby_llm/error_middleware'
     7	require 'timeout'
     8	
     9	module RubyLLM
    10	  # Connection class for managing API connections to various providers.
    11	  class Connection
    12	    attr_reader :provider, :connection, :config
    13	
    14	    def self.basic(&)
    15	      Faraday.new do |f|
    16	        f.response :logger,
    17	                   RubyLLM.logger,
    18	                   bodies: false,
    19	                   errors: true,
    20	                   headers: false,
    21	                   log_level: :debug
    22	        f.response :raise_error
    23	        yield f if block_given?
    24	      end
    25	    end
    26	
    27	    def initialize(provider, config)
    28	      @provider = provider
    29	      @config = config
    30	
    31	      @connection = Faraday.new(provider.api_base) do |faraday|
    32	        setup_timeout(faraday)
    33	        setup_logging(faraday)
    34	        setup_retry(faraday)
    35	        setup_middleware(faraday)
    36	        setup_http_proxy(faraday)
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    def post(url, payload, &)
    41	      instrument_request(:post, url) do
    42	        @connection.post url, payload do |req|
    43	          req.headers.merge! @provider.headers
    44	          yield req if block_given?
    45	        end
    46	      end
    47	    end
    48	
    49	    def get(url, &)
    50	      instrument_request(:get, url) do
    51	        @connection.get url do |req|
    52	          req.headers.merge! @provider.headers
    53	          yield req if block_given?
    54	        end
    55	      end
    56	    end
    57	
    58	    # Keeps the config and Faraday internals out of pretty-printed output.
    59	    def pretty_print_instance_variables
    60	      super - %i[@config @connection]
    61	    end
    62	
    63	    private
    64	
    65	    def instrument_request(method, url)
    66	      payload = {
    67	        provider: @provider.slug,
    68	        method: method,
    69	        url: url
    70	      }
    71	
    72	      RubyLLM.instrument('request.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do
    73	        response = yield
    74	        payload[:status] = response.status if response.respond_to?(:status)
    75	        response
    76	      end
    77	    end
    78	
    79	    def setup_timeout(faraday)
    80	      faraday.options.timeout = @config.request_timeout
    81	    end
    82	
    83	    def setup_logging(faraday)
    84	      faraday.response :logger,
    85	                       RubyLLM.logger,
    86	                       bodies: RubyLLM.logger.debug?,
    87	                       errors: true,
    88	                       headers: false,
    89	                       log_level: :debug do |logger|
    90	        logger.filter(logging_regexp('[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{100,}'), '[BASE64 DATA]')
    91	        logger.filter(logging_regexp('[-\\d.e,\\s]{100,}'), '[EMBEDDINGS ARRAY]')
    92	      end
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    def logging_regexp(pattern)
    96	      return Regexp.new(pattern) if @config.log_regexp_timeout.nil? || !Regexp.respond_to?(:timeout)
    97	
    98	      Regexp.new(pattern, timeout: @config.log_regexp_timeout)
    99	    end
   100	
   101	    def setup_retry(faraday)
   102	      faraday.request :retry, {
   103	        max: @config.max_retries,
   104	        interval: @config.retry_interval,
   105	        interval_randomness: @config.retry_interval_randomness,
   106	        backoff_factor: @config.retry_backoff_factor,
   107	        methods: Faraday::Retry::Middleware::IDEMPOTENT_METHODS + [:post],
   108	        exceptions: retry_exceptions
   109	      }
   110	    end
   111	
   112	    def setup_middleware(faraday)
   113	      faraday.request :multipart
   114	      faraday.request :json
   115	      faraday.response :json
   116	      faraday.adapter(@config.faraday_adapter)
   117	      faraday.use :llm_errors, provider: @provider
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    def setup_http_proxy(faraday)
   121	      return unless @config.http_proxy
   122	
   123	      faraday.proxy = @config.http_proxy
   124	    end
   125	
   126	    def retry_exceptions
   127	      [
   128	        Errno::ETIMEDOUT,
   129	        Timeout::Error,
   130	        Faraday::TimeoutError,
   131	        Faraday::ConnectionFailed,
   132	        Faraday::RetriableResponse,
   133	        RubyLLM::RateLimitError,
   134	        RubyLLM::ServerError,
   135	        RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError,
   136	        RubyLLM::OverloadedError
   137	      ]
   138	    end
   139	  end
   140	end

     2	
     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  # Base class for wire protocols. A protocol knows how to talk to a family
     5	  # of APIs: rendering payloads, parsing responses, streaming chunks, and the
     6	  # endpoints involved. Providers know where to talk and who they are.
     7	  class Protocol
     8	    include Streaming
     9	
    10	    attr_reader :provider, :config, :connection, :model
    11	
    12	    def initialize(provider, model = nil)
    13	      @provider = provider
    14	      @config = provider.config
    15	      @connection = provider.connection
    16	      @model = model
    17	    end
    18	
    19	    # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
    20	    def complete(messages, tools:, temperature:, params: {}, headers: {}, schema: nil, thinking: nil,
    21	                 citations: false, tool_prefs: nil, &)
    22	      normalized_temperature = maybe_normalize_temperature(temperature, model)
    23	
    24	      payload = Utils.deep_merge(
    25	        render_payload(
    26	          messages,
    27	          tools: tools,
    28	          tool_prefs: tool_prefs,
    29	          temperature: normalized_temperature,
    30	          model: model,
    31	          stream: block_given?,
    32	          schema: schema,
    33	          thinking: thinking,
    34	          citations: citations
    35	        ),
    36	        params
    37	      )
    38	
    39	      if block_given?
    40	        stream_response payload, headers, &
    41	      else
    42	        sync_response payload, headers
    43	      end
    44	    end
    45	    # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
    46	
    47	    def list_models
    48	      response = @connection.get models_url
    49	      parse_list_models_response response, @provider.slug, @provider.capabilities
    50	    end
    51	
    52	    def embed(text, model:, dimensions:)
    53	      payload = render_embedding_payload(text, model:, dimensions:)
    54	      response = @connection.post(embedding_url(model:), payload)
    55	      parse_embedding_response(response, model:, text:)
    56	    end
    57	
    58	    def moderate(input, model:)
    59	      payload = render_moderation_payload(input, model:)
    60	      response = @connection.post moderation_url, payload
    61	      parse_moderation_response(response, model:)
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    def paint(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
    65	      validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
    66	      payload = render_image_payload(prompt, model:, size:, with:, mask:, params:)
    67	      response = @connection.post images_url(with:, mask:), payload
    68	      parse_image_response(response, model:)
    69	    end
    70	
    71	    def transcribe(audio_file, model:, language:, **options)
    72	      file_part = build_audio_file_part(audio_file)
    73	      payload = render_transcription_payload(file_part, model:, language:, **options)
    74	      response = @connection.post transcription_url, payload
    75	      parse_transcription_response(response, model:)
    76	    end
    77	
    78	    def maybe_normalize_temperature(temperature, _model)
    79	      temperature
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    def parse_error(response)
    83	      @provider.parse_error(response)
    84	    end
    85	
    86	    private
    87	
    88	    def validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
    89	      return if with.nil? && mask.nil?
    90	
    91	      raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, 'image reference'
    92	    end
    93	
    94	    def build_audio_file_part(file_path)
    95	      require 'faraday/multipart'
    96	      require 'marcel'
    97	      require 'pathname'
    98	
    99	      expanded_path = File.expand_path(file_path)
   100	      mime_type = Marcel::MimeType.for(Pathname.new(expanded_path))
   101	
   102	      Faraday::Multipart::FilePart.new(
   103	        expanded_path,
   104	        mime_type,
   105	        File.basename(expanded_path)
   106	      )
   107	    end
   108	
   109	    def sync_response(payload, additional_headers = {})
   110	      response = @connection.post completion_url, payload do |req|
   111	        req.headers = additional_headers.merge(req.headers) unless additional_headers.empty?
   112	      end
   113	      parse_completion_response response
   114	    end
   115	  end
   116	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	require 'logger'
     4	
     5	module RubyLLM
     6	  # Global configuration for RubyLLM
     7	  class Configuration
     8	    class << self
     9	      # Declare a single configuration option.
    10	      def option(key, default = nil)
    11	        key = key.to_sym
    12	        return if options.include?(key)
    13	
    14	        attr_reader key
    15	
    16	        define_method("#{key}=") do |value|
    17	          value = nil if value.is_a?(String) && value.strip.empty?
    18	          instance_variable_set(:"@#{key}", value)
    19	        end
    20	
    21	        option_keys << key
    22	        defaults[key] = default
    23	      end
    24	
    25	      def register_provider_options(options)
    26	        Array(options).each { |key| option(key, nil) }
    27	      end
    28	
    29	      def options
    30	        option_keys.dup
    31	      end
    32	
    33	      private
    34	
    35	      def option_keys = @option_keys ||= []
    36	      def defaults = @defaults ||= {}
    37	      private :option
    38	    end
    39	
    40	    # System-level options are declared here.
    41	    # Provider-specific options are declared in each provider class via
    42	    # `self.configuration_options` and registered through Provider.register.
    43	    option :default_model, 'gpt-5.4'
    44	    option :default_embedding_model, 'text-embedding-3-small'
    45	    option :default_moderation_model, 'omni-moderation-latest'
    46	    option :default_image_model, 'gpt-image-1.5'
    47	    option :default_transcription_model, 'whisper-1'
    48	
    49	    option :model_registry_file, -> { File.expand_path('models.json', __dir__) }
    50	    option :model_registry_class, 'Model'
    51	
    52	    option :use_new_acts_as, true
    53	    option :model_registry_source, nil
    54	
    55	    option :request_timeout, 300
    56	    option :max_retries, 3
    57	    option :retry_interval, 0.1
    58	    option :retry_backoff_factor, 2
    59	    option :retry_interval_randomness, 0.5
    60	    option :http_proxy, nil
    61	    option :tool_concurrency, false
    62	
    63	    option :logger, nil
    64	    option :instrumenter, nil
    65	    option :deprecation_behavior, :warn
    66	    option :faraday_adapter, :net_http
    67	    option :log_file, -> { $stdout }
    68	    option :log_level, -> { ENV['RUBYLLM_DEBUG'] ? Logger::DEBUG : Logger::INFO }
    69	    option :log_stream_debug, -> { ENV['RUBYLLM_STREAM_DEBUG'] == 'true' }
    70	    option :log_regexp_timeout, -> { Regexp.respond_to?(:timeout) ? (Regexp.timeout || 1.0) : nil }
    71	
    72	    def initialize
    73	      self.class.send(:defaults).each do |key, default|
    74	        value = default.respond_to?(:call) ? instance_exec(&default) : default
    75	        public_send("#{key}=", value)
    76	      end
    77	    end
    78	
    79	    def instance_variables
    80	      super.reject { |ivar| ivar.to_s.match?(/(_id|_key|_secret|_token)$/) }
    81	    end
    82	
    83	    def log_regexp_timeout=(value)
    84	      if value && !Regexp.respond_to?(:timeout)
    85	        RubyLLM.logger.warn("log_regexp_timeout is not supported on Ruby #{RUBY_VERSION}")
    86	      end
    87	      @log_regexp_timeout = value
    88	    end
    89	  end
    90	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module RubyLLM
     4	  # Custom error class that wraps API errors from different providers
     5	  # into a consistent format with helpful error messages.
     6	  class Error < StandardError
     7	    attr_reader :response
     8	
     9	    def self.default_message
    10	      nil
    11	    end
    12	
    13	    def initialize(response = nil, message = nil)
    14	      if response.is_a?(String)
    15	        message = response
    16	        response = nil
    17	      end
    18	
    19	      @response = response
    20	      super(message || response&.body || self.class.default_message)
    21	    end
    22	  end
    23	
    24	  # Error classes for non-HTTP errors
    25	  class ConfigurationError < StandardError; end
    26	  class PromptNotFoundError < StandardError; end
    27	  class InvalidRoleError < StandardError; end
    28	  class InvalidToolChoiceError < StandardError; end
    29	  class ModelNotFoundError < StandardError; end
    30	
    31	  # Raised when RubyLLM cannot format an attachment for the selected provider.
    32	  class UnsupportedAttachmentError < StandardError
    33	    GUIDANCE = 'Consider using a model that supports this attachment type.'
    34	
    35	    def initialize(type = nil)
    36	      message = 'Unsupported attachment type'
    37	      message = "#{message}: #{type}" if type
    38	      super("#{message}. #{GUIDANCE}")
    39	    end
    40	  end
    41	
    42	  # Error classes for different HTTP status codes
    43	  # Raised when the API request is invalid.
    44	  class BadRequestError < Error
    45	    def self.default_message
    46	      'Invalid request - please check your input'
    47	    end
    48	  end
    49	
    50	  # Raised when the API key or account lacks permission for the request.
    51	  class ForbiddenError < Error
    52	    def self.default_message
    53	      'Forbidden - you do not have permission to access this resource'
    54	    end
    55	  end
    56	
    57	  # Raised when token or context limits are exceeded for a request.
    58	  class ContextLengthExceededError < Error
    59	    def self.default_message
    60	      'Context length exceeded'
    61	    end
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  # Raised when the service signals temporary overload.
    65	  class OverloadedError < Error
    66	    def self.default_message
    67	      'Service overloaded - please try again later'
    68	    end
    69	  end
    70	
    71	  # Raised when account billing prevents the request from being completed.
    72	  class PaymentRequiredError < Error
    73	    def self.default_message
    74	      'Payment required - please top up your account'
    75	    end
    76	  end
    77	
    78	  # Raised when rate limits are exceeded.
    79	  class RateLimitError < Error
    80	    def self.default_message
    81	      'Rate limit exceeded - please wait a moment'
    82	    end
    83	  end
    84	
    85	  # Raised when the provider returns a server-side error.
    86	  class ServerError < Error
    87	    def self.default_message
    88	      'API server error - please try again'
    89	    end
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  # Raised when the provider is temporarily unavailable.
    93	  class ServiceUnavailableError < Error
    94	    def self.default_message
    95	      'API server unavailable - please try again later'
    96	    end
    97	  end
    98	
    99	  # Raised when authentication fails.
   100	  class UnauthorizedError < Error
   101	    def self.default_message
   102	      'Invalid API key - check your credentials'
   103	    end
   104	  end
   105	end

Settings are registered dynamically: provider classes declare option names, `Provider.register` installs them on `Configuration`, and provider construction validates requirements. I’m also tracing failure classification through the Faraday middleware so error handling customizations are covered, not just the provider parse hooks.

     2	
     3	require 'faraday'
     4	require 'ruby_llm/error'
     5	
     6	module RubyLLM
     7	  # Faraday middleware that maps provider-specific API errors to RubyLLM errors.
     8	  class ErrorMiddleware < Faraday::Middleware
     9	    def initialize(app, options = {})
    10	      super(app)
    11	      @provider = options[:provider]
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    def call(env)
    15	      @app.call(env).on_complete do |response|
    16	        self.class.parse_error(provider: @provider, response: response)
    17	      end
    18	    end
    19	
    20	    class << self
    21	      CONTEXT_LENGTH_PATTERNS = [
    22	        /context length/i,
    23	        /context window/i,
    24	        /maximum context/i,
    25	        /request too large/i,
    26	        /too many tokens/i,
    27	        /token count exceeds/i,
    28	        /input[_\s-]?token/i,
    29	        /input or output tokens? must be reduced/i,
    30	        /reduce the length of messages/i,
    31	        /prompt is too long/i
    32	      ].freeze
    33	
    34	      RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS = [
    35	        /rate limit/i,
    36	        /per minute/i,
    37	        /per hour/i,
    38	        /per day/i
    39	      ].freeze
    40	
    41	      def parse_error(provider:, response:)
    42	        message = provider&.parse_error(response)
    43	
    44	        case response.status
    45	        when 200..399
    46	          message
    47	        when 400
    48	          raise ContextLengthExceededError.new(response, message) if context_length_exceeded?(message)
    49	
    50	          raise BadRequestError.new(response, message)
    51	        when 401
    52	          raise UnauthorizedError.new(response, message)
    53	        when 402
    54	          raise PaymentRequiredError.new(response, message)
    55	        when 403
    56	          raise ForbiddenError.new(response, message)
    57	        when 429
    58	          raise RateLimitError.new(response, message) if rate_limited?(message)
    59	          raise ContextLengthExceededError.new(response, message) if context_length_exceeded?(message)
    60	
    61	          raise RateLimitError.new(response, message)
    62	        when 500
    63	          raise ServerError.new(response, message)
    64	        when 502..504
    65	          raise ServiceUnavailableError.new(response, message)
    66	        when 529
    67	          raise OverloadedError.new(response, message)
    68	        else
    69	          raise Error.new(response, message)
    70	        end
    71	      end
    72	
    73	      private
    74	
    75	      def context_length_exceeded?(message)
    76	        return false if message.to_s.empty?
    77	
    78	        CONTEXT_LENGTH_PATTERNS.any? { |pattern| message.match?(pattern) }
    79	      end
    80	
    81	      def rate_limited?(message)
    82	        return false if message.to_s.empty?
    83	
    84	        RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS.any? { |pattern| message.match?(pattern) }
    85	      end
    86	    end
    87	  end
    88	end
    89	
    90	Faraday::Middleware.register_middleware(llm_errors: RubyLLM::ErrorMiddleware)

     4	require 'faraday'
     5	require 'json'
     6	
     7	module RubyLLM
     8	  # Handles streaming responses from AI providers.
     9	  module Streaming
    10	    module_function
    11	
    12	    def stream_response(payload, additional_headers = {}, &block)
    13	      accumulator = StreamAccumulator.new
    14	      on_data = handle_stream do |chunk|
    15	        accumulator.add chunk
    16	        block.call chunk
    17	      end
    18	
    19	      response = @connection.post stream_url, payload do |req|
    20	        req.headers = additional_headers.merge(req.headers) unless additional_headers.empty?
    21	        if faraday_1?
    22	          req.options[:on_data] = on_data
    23	        else
    24	          req.options.on_data = on_data
    25	        end
    26	      end
    27	
    28	      message = accumulator.to_message(response)
    29	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Stream completed: #{message.content}" }
    30	      message
    31	    end
    32	
    33	    def handle_stream(&block)
    34	      build_on_data_handler do |data|
    35	        block.call(build_chunk(data)) if data.is_a?(Hash)
    36	      end
    37	    end
    38	
    39	    private
    40	
    41	    def faraday_1?
    42	      Faraday::VERSION.start_with?('1')
    43	    end
    44	
    45	    def build_on_data_handler(&handler)
    46	      buffer = +''
    47	      parser = EventStreamParser::Parser.new
    48	
    49	      FaradayHandlers.build(
    50	        faraday_v1: faraday_1?,
    51	        on_chunk: ->(chunk, env) { process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &handler) },
    52	        on_failed_response: ->(chunk, env) { handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env) }
    53	      )
    54	    end
    55	
    56	    def process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &)
    57	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Received chunk: #{chunk}" } if RubyLLM.config.log_stream_debug
    58	
    59	      if error_chunk?(chunk)
    60	        handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
    61	      elsif json_error_payload?(chunk)
    62	        handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
    63	      else
    64	        handle_sse(chunk, parser, env, &)
    65	      end
    66	    end
    67	
    68	    def error_chunk?(chunk)
    69	      chunk.start_with?('event: error')
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    def json_error_payload?(chunk)
    73	      chunk.lstrip.start_with?('{') && chunk.include?('"error"')
    74	    end
    75	
    76	    def handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
    77	      parse_error_from_json(chunk, env, 'Failed to parse JSON error chunk')
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    def handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
    81	      error_data = chunk.split("\n")[1].delete_prefix('data: ')
    82	      parse_error_from_json(error_data, env, 'Failed to parse error chunk')
    83	    end
    84	
    85	    def handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env)
    86	      buffer << chunk
    87	      error_data = JSON.parse(buffer)
    88	      raise_stream_error(buffer, error_data, env)
    89	    rescue JSON::ParserError
    90	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Accumulating error chunk: #{chunk}" }
    91	    end
    92	
    93	    def handle_sse(chunk, parser, env)
    94	      parser.feed(chunk) do |type, data|
    95	        case type.to_sym
    96	        when :error
    97	          handle_error_event(data, env)
    98	        else
    99	          yield handle_data(data, env) unless data == '[DONE]'
   100	        end
   101	      end
   102	    end
   103	
   104	    def handle_data(data, env)
   105	      parsed = JSON.parse(data)
   106	      return parsed unless parsed.is_a?(Hash) && parsed.key?('error')
   107	
   108	      raise_stream_error(data, parsed, env)
   109	    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
   110	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse data chunk: #{e.message}" }
   111	    end
   112	
   113	    def handle_error_event(data, env)
   114	      parse_error_from_json(data, env, 'Failed to parse error event')
   115	    end
   116	
   117	    def parse_streaming_error(data)
   118	      error_data = JSON.parse(data)
   119	      [500, error_data['message'] || 'Unknown streaming error']
   120	    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
   121	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse streaming error: #{e.message}" }
   122	      [500, "Failed to parse error: #{data}"]
   123	    end
   124	
   125	    def raise_stream_error(raw_data, parsed_data, env)
   126	      status, _message = parse_streaming_error(raw_data)
   127	      error_response = build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
   128	      ErrorMiddleware.parse_error(provider: self, response: error_response)
   129	    end
   130	
   131	    def parse_error_from_json(data, env, error_message)
   132	      parsed_data = JSON.parse(data)
   133	      raise_stream_error(data, parsed_data, env)
   134	    rescue JSON::ParserError => e
   135	      RubyLLM.logger.debug { "#{error_message}: #{e.message}" }
   136	    end
   137	
   138	    def build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
   139	      error_status = status || env&.status || 500
   140	
   141	      if faraday_1?
   142	        Struct.new(:body, :status).new(parsed_data, error_status)
   143	      else
   144	        env.merge(body: parsed_data, status: error_status)
   145	      end
   146	    end
   147	
   148	    # Builds Faraday on_data handlers for different major versions.
   149	    module FaradayHandlers
   150	      module_function
   151	
   152	      def build(faraday_v1:, on_chunk:, on_failed_response:)
   153	        if faraday_v1
   154	          v1_on_data(on_chunk)
   155	        else
   156	          v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
   157	        end
   158	      end
   159	
   160	      def v1_on_data(on_chunk)
   161	        proc do |chunk, _size|
   162	          on_chunk.call(chunk, nil)
   163	        end
   164	      end
   165	
   166	      def v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
   167	        proc do |chunk, _bytes, env|
   168	          if env&.status == 200
   169	            on_chunk.call(chunk, env)
   170	          else
   171	            on_failed_response.call(chunk, env)
   172	          end
   173	        end
   174	      end
   175	    end
   176	  end
   177	end

320	        return value.empty? if value.is_a?(String) || value.is_a?(Array)
   321	
   322	        if value.is_a?(Hash)
   323	          return true if value.empty?
   324	
   325	          return value.values.all? { |nested| blank_value?(nested) }
   326	        end
   327	
   328	        false
   329	      end
   330	
   331	      def models_dev_model_to_info(model_data, provider_slug, provider_key)
   332	        modalities = normalize_models_dev_modalities(model_data[:modalities])
   333	        capabilities = models_dev_capabilities(model_data, modalities)
   334	
   335	        created_date = [model_data[:release_date], model_data[:last_updated]]
   336	                       .find { |value| !value.to_s.strip.empty? }
   337	
   338	        data = {
   339	          id: model_data[:id],
   340	          name: model_data[:name] || model_data[:id],
   341	          provider: provider_slug,
   342	          family: model_data[:family],
   343	          created_at: Utils.iso_date_prefix_to_utc_midnight_string(created_date),
   344	          context_window: model_data.dig(:limit, :context),
   345	          max_output_tokens: model_data.dig(:limit, :output),
   346	          knowledge_cutoff: normalize_models_dev_knowledge(model_data[:knowledge]),
   347	          modalities: modalities,
   348	          capabilities: capabilities,
   349	          pricing: models_dev_pricing(model_data[:cost]),
   350	          metadata: models_dev_metadata(model_data, provider_key)
   351	        }
   352	
   353	        normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
   354	        data
   355	      end
   356	
   357	      def models_dev_capabilities(model_data, modalities)
   358	        capabilities = []
   359	        capabilities << 'function_calling' if model_data[:tool_call]
   360	        capabilities << 'structured_output' if model_data[:structured_output]
   361	        capabilities << 'reasoning' if model_data[:reasoning] || model_data[:reasoning_options]
   362	        capabilities << 'vision' if modalities[:input].intersect?(%w[image video pdf])
   363	        capabilities.uniq
   364	      end
   365	
   366	      def models_dev_pricing(cost)
   367	        return {} unless cost
   368	
   369	        text_standard = {
   370	          input_per_million: cost[:input],
   371	          output_per_million: cost[:output],
   372	          cache_read_input_per_million: cost[:cache_read],
   373	          cache_write_input_per_million: cost[:cache_write],
   374	          reasoning_output_per_million: cost[:reasoning]
   375	        }.compact
   376	
   377	        audio_standard = {
   378	          input_per_million: cost[:input_audio],
   379	          output_per_million: cost[:output_audio]
   380	        }.compact
   381	
   382	        pricing = {}
   383	        pricing[:text_tokens] = { standard: text_standard } if text_standard.any?
   384	        pricing[:audio_tokens] = { standard: audio_standard } if audio_standard.any?
   385	        pricing
   386	      end
   387	
   388	      def models_dev_metadata(model_data, provider_key)
   389	        metadata = {
   390	          source: 'models.dev',
   391	          provider_id: provider_key,
   392	          open_weights: model_data[:open_weights],
   393	          attachment: model_data[:attachment],
   394	          temperature: model_data[:temperature],
   395	          last_updated: model_data[:last_updated],
   396	          status: model_data[:status],
   397	          interleaved: model_data[:interleaved],
   398	          reasoning_options: model_data[:reasoning_options],
   399	          cost: model_data[:cost],
   400	          limit: model_data[:limit],
   401	          knowledge: model_data[:knowledge]
   402	        }
   403	        metadata.compact
   404	      end
   405	
   406	      def normalize_models_dev_modalities(modalities)
   407	        normalized = { input: [], output: [] }
   408	        return normalized unless modalities
   409	
   410	        normalized[:input] = Array(modalities[:input]).compact & MODELS_DEV_INPUT_MODALITIES
   411	        normalized[:output] = Array(modalities[:output]).compact & MODELS_DEV_OUTPUT_MODALITIES
   412	        normalized
   413	      end
   414	
   415	      def normalize_models_dev_knowledge(value)
   416	        return if value.nil?
   417	        return value if value.is_a?(Date)
   418	
   419	        Date.parse(value.to_s)
   420	      rescue ArgumentError
   421	        nil
   422	      end
   423	    end
   424	
   425	    def initialize(models = nil)
   426	      @models = self.class.filter_models(models || self.class.load_models)
   427	    end
   428	
   429	    # Replaces this registry instance with models loaded from JSON.
   430	    def load_from_json!(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
   431	      @models = self.class.read_from_json(file)
   432	    end
   433	
   434	    # Replaces this registry instance with models loaded from the configured
   435	    # ActiveRecord model class.
   436	    def load_from_database!
   437	      @models = self.class.read_from_database
   438	    end
   439	
   440	    # Persists this registry instance to JSON without changing the global
   441	    # RubyLLM.models instance.
   442	    def save_to_json(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
   443	      File.write(file, JSON.pretty_generate(all.map(&:to_h)))
   444	    end
   445	
   446	    def all
   447	      @models
   448	    end
   449	
   450	    def each(&)
   451	      all.each(&)
   452	    end
   453	
   454	    def find(model_id, provider = nil)
   455	      if provider
   456	        find_with_provider(model_id, provider)
   457	      else
   458	        find_without_provider(model_id)
   459	      end
   460	    end

     3	require 'spec_helper'
