     5	RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Models do
     6	  include_context 'with configured RubyLLM'
     7	
     8	  describe 'models.json structure validation' do
     9	    let(:models_json_path) { RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file }
    10	    let(:models_data) { JSON.parse(File.read(models_json_path)) }
    11	
    12	    it 'validates models.json has correct structure' do
    13	      expect(models_data).to be_an(Array)
    14	      expect(models_data).not_to be_empty
    15	
    16	      models_data.each do |model|
    17	        # Required fields
    18	        expect(model).to have_key('id')
    19	        expect(model['id']).to be_a(String)
    20	
    21	        expect(model).to have_key('name')
    22	        expect(model['name']).to be_a(String)
    23	
    24	        expect(model).to have_key('provider')
    25	        expect(model['provider']).to be_a(String)
    26	
    27	        # Optional fields with type validation
    28	        expect(model['family']).to be_a(String).or be_nil if model.key?('family')
    29	
    30	        expect(model['created_at']).to be_a(String).or be_nil if model.key?('created_at')
    31	
    32	        expect(model['context_window']).to be_a(Integer).or be_nil if model.key?('context_window')
    33	
    34	        expect(model['max_output_tokens']).to be_a(Integer).or be_nil if model.key?('max_output_tokens')
    35	
    36	        expect(model['knowledge_cutoff']).to be_a(String).or be_nil if model.key?('knowledge_cutoff')
    37	
    38	        if model.key?('modalities')
    39	          expect(model['modalities']).to be_a(Hash)
    40	          if model['modalities'].key?('input')
    41	            expect(model['modalities']['input']).to be_an(Array)
    42	            expect(model['modalities']['input']).to all(be_a(String))
    43	          end
    44	          if model['modalities'].key?('output')
    45	            expect(model['modalities']['output']).to be_an(Array)
    46	            expect(model['modalities']['output']).to all(be_a(String))
    47	          end
    48	        end
    49	
    50	        if model.key?('capabilities')
    51	          expect(model['capabilities']).to be_an(Array)
    52	          expect(model['capabilities']).to all(be_a(String))
    53	        end
    54	
    55	        expect(model['pricing']).to be_a(Hash) if model.key?('pricing')
    56	
    57	        expect(model['metadata']).to be_a(Hash) if model.key?('metadata')
    58	      end
    59	    end
    60	
    61	    it 'ensures all models have capabilities as an array' do
    62	      models_data.each do |model|
    63	        expect(model['capabilities']).to be_an(Array),
    64	                                         "Model #{model['id']} (#{model['provider']}) has capabilities as " \
    65	                                         "#{model['capabilities'].class} instead of Array"
    66	      end
    67	    end
    68	
    69	    it 'ensures no provider has models with mixed capability types' do
    70	      providers_with_issues = {}
    71	
    72	      models_data.group_by { |m| m['provider'] }.each do |provider, models|
    73	        capability_types = models.map { |m| m['capabilities'].class }.uniq
    74	        providers_with_issues[provider] = capability_types if capability_types.size > 1
    75	      end
    76	
    77	      expect(providers_with_issues).to be_empty,
    78	                                       "Providers with mixed capability types: #{providers_with_issues}"
    79	    end
    80	  end
    81	
    82	  describe 'refresh models output structure' do

    96	    after do

    99	
   100	    let(:mock_provider_models) do

    78	        provider: RubyLLM::Providers::XAI,
    79	        key: :xai_api_base,
    80	        custom: 'https://xai-proxy.example.com/v1',
    81	        default: 'https://api.x.ai/v1'

    86	  def config_for(slug)
    87	    RubyLLM::Configuration.new.tap do |config|
    88	      case slug
    89	      when :anthropic
    90	        config.anthropic_api_key = 'anthropic-key'
    91	      when :azure
    92	        config.azure_api_key = 'azure-key'
    93	      when :bedrock
    94	        config.bedrock_api_key = 'bedrock-key'
    95	        config.bedrock_secret_key = 'bedrock-secret'
    96	        config.bedrock_region = 'us-east-1'
    97	      when :deepseek
    98	        config.deepseek_api_key = 'deepseek-key'
    99	      when :gemini
   100	        config.gemini_api_key = 'gemini-key'
   101	      when :gpustack
   102	        config.gpustack_api_key = 'gpustack-key'
   103	      when :mistral
   104	        config.mistral_api_key = 'mistral-key'
   105	      when :ollama
   106	        config.ollama_api_key = 'ollama-key'
   107	      when :openai
   108	        config.openai_api_key = 'openai-key'
   109	      when :openrouter
   110	        config.openrouter_api_key = 'openrouter-key'
   111	      when :perplexity
   112	        config.perplexity_api_key = 'perplexity-key'
   113	      when :vertexai
   114	        config.vertexai_project_id = 'vertex-project'
   115	        config.vertexai_location = 'us-east1'
   116	      when :xai

   122	  describe '.register' do
   123	    it 'registers provider configuration options on Configuration' do
   124	      provider_key = :test_provider_spec
   125	      option_keys = %i[test_provider_api_key test_provider_api_base]
   126	
   127	      provider_class = Class.new(described_class) do
   128	        class << self
   129	          def configuration_options
   130	            %i[test_provider_api_key test_provider_api_base]
   131	          end
   132	
   133	          def configuration_requirements
   134	            %i[test_provider_api_key]
   135	          end
   136	        end
   137	      end
   138	
   139	      original_providers = described_class.providers.dup
   140	
   141	      begin
   142	        described_class.register(provider_key, provider_class)
   143	
   144	        config = RubyLLM::Configuration.new
   145	        option_keys.each do |key|
   146	          expect(config).to respond_to(key)
   147	          expect(config).to respond_to("#{key}=")
   148	        end
   149	      ensure
   150	        described_class.providers.replace(original_providers)
   151	        option_keys.each do |key|
   152	          RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:option_keys).delete(key)
   153	          RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:defaults).delete(key)
   154	          RubyLLM::Configuration.class_eval do
   155	            remove_method key if method_defined?(key)
   156	            remove_method :"#{key}=" if method_defined?(:"#{key}=")
   157	          end
   158	        end
   159	      end
   160	    end
   161	  end
   162	
   163	  describe '.configured?' do
   164	    it 'treats blank required configuration as missing' do
   165	      provider_class = Class.new(described_class) do
   166	        class << self
   167	          def configuration_options
   168	            %i[blank_test_api_key]
   169	          end
   170	
   171	          def configuration_requirements
   172	            %i[blank_test_api_key]
   173	          end
   174	        end
   175	      end
   176	
   177	      RubyLLM::Configuration.register_provider_options(provider_class.configuration_options)
   178	      config = RubyLLM::Configuration.new
   179	      config.blank_test_api_key = ''
   180	
   181	      expect(provider_class.configured?(config)).to be(false)
   182	    ensure
   183	      RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:option_keys).delete(:blank_test_api_key)
   184	      RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:defaults).delete(:blank_test_api_key)
   185	      RubyLLM::Configuration.class_eval do
   186	        remove_method :blank_test_api_key if method_defined?(:blank_test_api_key)
   187	        remove_method :blank_test_api_key= if method_defined?(:blank_test_api_key=)
   188	      end
   189	    end
   190	  end

   192	  describe 'provider configuration schema' do
   193	    it 'keeps requirements as a subset of declared configuration options' do
   194	      described_class.providers.each_value do |provider_class|
   195	        missing = provider_class.configuration_requirements - provider_class.configuration_options
   196	        expect(missing).to be_empty, "#{provider_class.name} is missing options for requirements: #{missing.inspect}"

   206

   243	  describe 'protocol resolution' do
   244	    let(:provider) { RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config_for(:openai)) }
   245	    let(:model) { instance_double(RubyLLM::Model::Info, id: 'gpt-5.4') }

260	    end

   264	      config.openai_protocol = :chat_completions

   268	      expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions)

   274	
   275	      provider = RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config)

    66	def refresh_models
    67	  existing_models = RubyLLM::Models.read_from_json
    68	  initial_count = existing_models.size
    69	  puts "Refreshing models (#{initial_count} cached)..."
    70	
    71	  models = RubyLLM.models.refresh!
    72	
    73	  if models.all.empty? && initial_count.zero?
    74	    puts 'Error: Failed to fetch models.'
    75	    exit(1)
    76	  else
    77	    existing_data = sorted_models_data(existing_models)
    78	    new_data = sorted_models_data(models.all)
    79	
    80	    if new_data == existing_data && initial_count.positive?
    81	      puts 'Warning: Model list unchanged.'
    82	    else
    83	      puts 'Validating models...'
    84	      validate_models!(models)
    85	
    86	      puts "Saving models.json (#{models.all.size} models)"
    87	      models.save_to_json
    88	    end
    89	  end
    90	
    91	  @models = models
    92	end
    93	
    94	def sorted_models_data(models)
    95	  models.map(&:to_h)
    96	        .sort_by { |model| [model[:provider].to_s, model[:id].to_s] }
    97	end
    98	
    99	def validate_models!(models)
   100	  schema_path = RubyLLM::Models.schema_file
   101	  models_data = models.all.map(&:to_h)
   102	
   103	  validation_errors = JSON::Validator.fully_validate(schema_path, models_data)
   104	
   105	  unless validation_errors.empty?
   106	    # Save failed models for inspection
   107	    failed_path = File.expand_path('../ruby_llm/models.failed.json', __dir__)
   108	    File.write(failed_path, JSON.pretty_generate(models_data))
   109	
   110	    puts 'ERROR: Models validation failed:'
   111	    puts "\nValidation errors:"
   112	    validation_errors.first(10).each { |error| puts "  - #{error}" }
   113	    puts "  ... and #{validation_errors.size - 10} more errors" if validation_errors.size > 10
   114	    puts "-> Failed models saved to: #{failed_path}"
   115	    exit(1)
   116	  end
   117	
   118	  puts '✓ Models validation passed'
   119	end
   120	
   121	def display_model_stats
   122	  puts "\nModel count:"
   123	  provider_counts = @models.all.group_by(&:provider).transform_values(&:count)
   124	
   125	  RubyLLM::Provider.providers.each do |sym, provider_class|
   126	    name = provider_class.name
   127	    count = provider_counts[sym.to_s] || 0
   128	    status = status(sym)
   129	    puts "  #{name}: #{count} models #{status}"
   130	  end
   131	
   132	  puts 'Refresh complete.'
   133	end
   134	
   135	def status(provider_sym)
   136	  provider_class = RubyLLM::Provider.providers[provider_sym]
   137	  if provider_class.local?
   138	    ' (LOCAL - SKIP)'
   139	  elsif provider_class.configured?(RubyLLM.config)
   140	    ' (OK)'
   141	  else
   142	    ' (NOT CONFIGURED)'
   143	  end
   144	end
   145	
   146	def generate_models_markdown
   147	  models = RubyLLM.models.all
   148	  total_models = models.count
   149	  provider_count = models.map(&:provider).uniq.count
   150	  generated_on = Time.now.utc.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

1	---
     2	layout: default
     3	title: Configuration
     4	nav_order: 3
     5	description: Configure once, use everywhere. API keys, defaults, timeouts, and multi-tenant contexts made simple.
     6	---
     7	
     8	# {{ page.title }}
     9	{: .no_toc }
    10	
    11	{{ page.description }}
    12	{: .fs-6 .fw-300 }
    13	
    14	## Table of contents
    15	{: .no_toc .text-delta }
    16	
    17	1. TOC
    18	{:toc}
    19	
    20	---
    21	
    22	After reading this guide, you will know:
    23	
    24	* How to configure API keys for different providers
    25	* How to set default models for chat, embeddings, and images
    26	* How to customize connection settings and timeouts
    27	* How to use custom endpoints and proxies
    28	* How to create isolated configurations with contexts
    29	* How to configure logging and debugging
    30	
    31	## Quick Start
    32	
    33	The simplest configuration just sets your API keys:
    34	
    35	```ruby
    36	RubyLLM.configure do |config|
    37	  config.openai_api_key = ENV['OPENAI_API_KEY']
    38	  config.anthropic_api_key = ENV['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    39	end
    40	```
    41	
    42	That's it. RubyLLM uses sensible defaults for everything else.
    43	
    44	## Provider Configuration
    45	
    46	### API Keys
    47	
    48	Configure API keys only for the providers you use. RubyLLM won't complain about missing keys for providers you never touch.
    49	
    50	```ruby
    51	RubyLLM.configure do |config|
    52	  # Anthropic
    53	  config.anthropic_api_key = ENV['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY']
    54	  config.anthropic_api_base = ENV['ANTHROPIC_API_BASE'] # Available in v1.13.0+ (optional custom Anthropic endpoint)
    55	
    56	  # Azure
    57	  config.azure_api_base = ENV['AZURE_API_BASE'] # Microsoft Foundry project endpoint
    58	  config.azure_api_key = ENV['AZURE_API_KEY'] # use this or
    59	  config.azure_ai_auth_token = ENV['AZURE_AI_AUTH_TOKEN'] # this
    60	
    61	  # Bedrock
    62	  config.bedrock_api_key = ENV['AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID']
    63	  config.bedrock_secret_key = ENV['AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY']
    64	  config.bedrock_region = ENV['AWS_REGION'] # Required for Bedrock
    65	  config.bedrock_session_token = ENV['AWS_SESSION_TOKEN'] # For temporary credentials
    66	  config.bedrock_api_base = ENV['BEDROCK_API_BASE'] # v1.16+ (optional custom Bedrock endpoint)
    67	
    68	  # DeepSeek
    69	  config.deepseek_api_key = ENV['DEEPSEEK_API_KEY']
    70	  config.deepseek_api_base = ENV['DEEPSEEK_API_BASE'] # Available in v1.13.0+ (optional custom DeepSeek endpoint)
    71	
    72	  # Gemini
    73	  config.gemini_api_key = ENV['GEMINI_API_KEY']
    74	  config.gemini_api_base = ENV['GEMINI_API_BASE'] # Available in v1.9.0+ (optional API version override)
    75	
    76	  # GPUStack
    77	  config.gpustack_api_base = ENV['GPUSTACK_API_BASE']
    78	  config.gpustack_api_key = ENV['GPUSTACK_API_KEY']
    79	
    80	  # Mistral
    81	  config.mistral_api_key = ENV['MISTRAL_API_KEY']
    82	  config.mistral_api_base = ENV['MISTRAL_API_BASE'] # v1.16+ (optional custom Mistral endpoint)
    83	
    84	  # Ollama
    85	  config.ollama_api_base = 'http://localhost:11434/v1'
    86	  config.ollama_api_key = ENV['OLLAMA_API_KEY'] # Available in v1.13.0+ (optional for authenticated/remote Ollama endpoints)
    87	
    88	  # OpenAI
    89	  config.openai_api_key = ENV['OPENAI_API_KEY']
    90	  config.openai_api_base = ENV['OPENAI_API_BASE'] # Optional custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint
    91	
    92	  # OpenRouter
    93	  config.openrouter_api_key = ENV['OPENROUTER_API_KEY']
    94	  config.openrouter_api_base = ENV['OPENROUTER_API_BASE'] # Available in v1.13.0+ (optional custom OpenRouter endpoint)
    95	
    96	  # Perplexity
    97	  config.perplexity_api_key = ENV['PERPLEXITY_API_KEY']
    98	  config.perplexity_api_base = ENV['PERPLEXITY_API_BASE'] # v1.16+ (optional custom Perplexity endpoint)
    99	
   100	  # Vertex AI
   101	  config.vertexai_project_id = ENV['GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT'] # Available in v1.7.0+
   102	  config.vertexai_location = ENV['GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION']
   103	  config.vertexai_service_account_key = ENV['VERTEXAI_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY'] # Optional: service account JSON key
   104	  config.vertexai_api_base = ENV['VERTEXAI_API_BASE'] # v1.16+ (optional custom Vertex AI endpoint)
   105	
   106	  # xAI
   107	  config.xai_api_key = ENV['XAI_API_KEY'] # Available in v1.11.0+
   108	  config.xai_api_base = ENV['XAI_API_BASE'] # v1.16+ (optional custom xAI endpoint)
   109	end
   110	```
   111	
   112	> Attempting to use an unconfigured provider will raise `RubyLLM::ConfigurationError`. Only configure what you need.
   113	{: .note }
   114	
   115	### OpenAI Organization & Project Headers
   116	
   117	For OpenAI users with multiple organizations or projects:
   118	
   119	```ruby
   120	RubyLLM.configure do |config|
   121	  config.openai_api_key = ENV['OPENAI_API_KEY']
   122	  config.openai_organization_id = ENV['OPENAI_ORG_ID']  # Billing organization
   123	  config.openai_project_id = ENV['OPENAI_PROJECT_ID']    # Usage tracking
   124	end
   125	```
   126	
   127	These headers are optional and only needed for organization-specific billing or project tracking.
   128	
   129	### Vertex AI Authentication Configuration
   130	
   131	RubyLLM supports both Vertex AI authentication methods:
   132	
   133	- Application Default Credentials (ADC)
   134	- Service Account JSON key via `config.vertexai_service_account_key`
   135	
   136	If `vertexai_service_account_key` is not set, RubyLLM uses ADC.
   137	
   138	## Custom Endpoints
   139	
   140	### OpenAI-Compatible APIs
   141	
   142	Connect to any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint, including local models, proxies, and custom servers:
   143	
   144	```ruby
   145	RubyLLM.configure do |config|
   146	  # API key - use what your server expects
   147	  config.openai_api_key = ENV['CUSTOM_API_KEY']  # Or 'dummy-key' if not required
   148	
   149	  # Your custom endpoint
   150	  config.openai_api_base = "http://localhost:8080/v1"  # vLLM, LiteLLM, etc.
   151	end
   152	
   153	# Use your custom model name
   154	chat = RubyLLM.chat(model: 'my-custom-model', provider: :openai, assume_model_exists: true)
   155	```
   156	
   157	#### System Role Compatibility
   158	
   159	OpenAI's API now uses 'developer' role for system messages, but some OpenAI-compatible servers still require the traditional 'system' role:
   160	
   161	```ruby
   162	RubyLLM.configure do |config|
   163	  # For servers that require 'system' role (e.g., older vLLM, some local models)
   164	  config.openai_use_system_role = true  # Use 'system' role instead of 'developer'
   165	
   166	  # Your OpenAI-compatible endpoint
   167	  config.openai_api_base = "http://localhost:11434/v1"  # Ollama, vLLM, etc.
   168	  config.openai_api_key = "dummy-key"  # If required by your server
   169	end
   170	```
   171	
   172	By default, RubyLLM uses the 'developer' role (matching OpenAI's current API). Set `openai_use_system_role` to true for compatibility with servers that still expect 'system'.
   173	
   174	### Gemini API Versions
   175	{: .d-inline-block }
   176	
   177	v1.9.0+
   178	{: .label .label-green }
   179	
   180	Gemini offers two API versions: `v1` (stable) and `v1beta` (early access). RubyLLM defaults to `v1beta` for access to the latest features, but you can switch to `v1` to support older models:
   181	
   182	```ruby
   183	RubyLLM.configure do |config|
   184	  config.gemini_api_key = ENV['GEMINI_API_KEY']
   185	  config.gemini_api_base = 'https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1'
   186	end
   187	```
   188	
   189	Some models are only available on specific API versions. For example, `gemini-1.5-flash-8b` requires `v1`. Check the [Gemini API documentation](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/api-versions) for version-specific model availability.
   190	
   191	### Provider-Specific API Base URLs
   192	{: .d-inline-block }
   193	
   194	v1.16+
   195	{: .label .label-green }
   196	
   197	Every provider exposes a provider-specific `*_api_base` setting in v1.16+. Use these when routing a native provider API through a proxy, gateway, private network endpoint, or compatible service:
   198	
   199	```ruby
   200	RubyLLM.configure do |config|
   201	  config.perplexity_api_base = ENV['PERPLEXITY_API_BASE']
   202	  config.mistral_api_base = ENV['MISTRAL_API_BASE']
   203	  config.xai_api_base = ENV['XAI_API_BASE']
   204	  config.bedrock_api_base = ENV['BEDROCK_API_BASE']
   205	  config.vertexai_api_base = ENV['VERTEXAI_API_BASE']
   206	end
   207	```
   208	
   209	Blank strings are treated as unset, so environment variables can be wired directly without causing invalid URL errors.
   210	
   211	## Default Models
   212	
   213	Set defaults for the convenience methods (`RubyLLM.chat`, `RubyLLM.embed`, `RubyLLM.paint`):
   214	
   215	```ruby
   216	RubyLLM.configure do |config|
   217	  config.default_model = '{{ site.models.anthropic_current }}'           # For RubyLLM.chat
   218	  config.default_embedding_model = '{{ site.models.embedding_large }}'  # For RubyLLM.embed
   219	  config.default_image_model = 'dall-e-3'              # For RubyLLM.paint
   220	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	require 'spec_helper'
     4	require 'json-schema'
     5	
     6	RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Models do
     7	  let(:schema_path) { described_class.schema_file }
     8	  let(:models_json_path) { RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file }
     9	
    10	  it 'validates that models.json conforms to the schema' do
    11	    models_data = JSON.parse(File.read(models_json_path))
    12	
    13	    validation_errors = JSON::Validator.fully_validate(schema_path, models_data)
    14	
    15	    expect(validation_errors).to be_empty,
    16	                                 "models.json has validation errors:\n#{validation_errors.join("\n")}"
    17	  end
    18	
    19	  it 'validates that all capabilities are arrays' do
    20	    models_data = JSON.parse(File.read(models_json_path))
    21	
    22	    models_with_non_array_capabilities = models_data.select do |model|
    23	      model['capabilities'] && !model['capabilities'].is_a?(Array)
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    expect(models_with_non_array_capabilities).to be_empty,
    27	                                                  'Models with non-array capabilities: ' \
    28	                                                  "#{models_with_non_array_capabilities.map { |m| m['id'] }.join(', ')}"
    29	  end
    30	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	require 'spec_helper'
     4	require 'tempfile'
     5	
     6	RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Models do
     7	  include_context 'with configured RubyLLM'
     8	
     9	  # Reset Models singleton after tests that modify it
    10	  after do
    11	    described_class.instance_variable_set(:@instance, nil)
    12	  end
    13	
    14	  describe 'filtering and chaining' do
    15	    it 'excludes slash-based vertexai models from the registry' do
    16	      models = described_class.new(
    17	        [
    18	          RubyLLM::Model::Info.new(id: 'deepseek-ai/deepseek-v3.1-maas', name: 'DeepSeek', provider: 'vertexai'),
    19	          RubyLLM::Model::Info.new(id: 'gemini-2.5-flash', name: 'Gemini', provider: 'vertexai'),
    20	          RubyLLM::Model::Info.new(id: 'deepseek-ai/deepseek-v3.1-maas', name: 'DeepSeek', provider: 'openrouter')
    21	        ]
    22	      )
    23	
    24	      expect(models.by_provider('vertexai').map(&:id)).to eq(['gemini-2.5-flash'])
    25	      expect(models.by_provider('openrouter').map(&:id)).to eq(['deepseek-ai/deepseek-v3.1-maas'])
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    it 'filters models by provider' do
    29	      openai_models = RubyLLM.models.by_provider('openai')
    30	      expect(openai_models.all).to all(have_attributes(provider: 'openai'))
    31	
    32	      # Can chain other filters and methods
    33	      expect(openai_models.chat_models).to be_a(described_class)
    34	    end
    35	
    36	    it 'chains filters in any order with same result' do
    37	      # These two filters should be equivalent
    38	      openai_chat_models = RubyLLM.models.by_provider('openai').chat_models
    39	      chat_openai_models = RubyLLM.models.chat_models.by_provider('openai')
    40	
    41	      # Both return same model IDs
    42	      expect(openai_chat_models.map(&:id).sort).to eq(chat_openai_models.map(&:id).sort)
    43	    end
    44	
    45	    it 'supports Enumerable methods' do
    46	      # Count models by provider
    47	      provider_counts = RubyLLM.models.group_by(&:provider)
    48	                               .transform_values(&:count)
    49	
    50	      # There should be models from at least OpenAI and Anthropic
    51	      expect(provider_counts.keys).to include('openai', 'anthropic')
    52	    end
    53	
    54	    it 'preserves class-level Enumerable delegation' do
    55	      openai_models = described_class.select { |model| model.provider == 'openai' }
    56	
    57	      expect(openai_models).to all(have_attributes(provider: 'openai'))
    58	      expect(described_class.count).to eq(RubyLLM.models.count)
    59	    end
    60	
    61	    it 'filters by vision support' do
    62	      vision_models = RubyLLM.models.select(&:supports_vision?)
    63	      expect(vision_models).to all(have_attributes(supports_vision?: true))
    64	    end
    65	
    66	    it 'filters by video support' do
    67	      video_models = RubyLLM.models.select(&:supports_video?)
    68	      expect(video_models).to all(have_attributes(supports_video?: true))
    69	    end
    70	  end
    71	
    72	  describe 'finding models' do
    73	    it 'finds models by ID' do
    74	      # Find the default model
    75	      model_id = RubyLLM.config.default_model
    76	      model = RubyLLM.models.find(model_id)
    77	      expect(model.id).to eq(model_id)
    78	
    79	      # Find a model with chaining
    80	      if RubyLLM.models.by_provider('openai').chat_models.any?
    81	        openai_chat_id = RubyLLM.models.by_provider('openai').chat_models.first.id
    82	        found = RubyLLM.models.by_provider('openai').find(openai_chat_id)
    83	        expect(found.id).to eq(openai_chat_id)
    84	        expect(found.provider).to eq('openai')
    85	      end
    86	    end
    87	
    88	    it 'raises ModelNotFoundError for unknown models' do
    89	      expect do
    90	        RubyLLM.models.find('nonexistent-model-12345')
    91	      end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ModelNotFoundError) { |error|
    92	        expect(error.message).to include('Unknown model: "nonexistent-model-12345"')
    93	        expect(error.message).to include('RubyLLM.models.refresh!')
    94	        expect(error.message).to include('RubyLLM.models.save_to_json')
    95	        expect(error.message).to include('Model.refresh!')
    96	        expect(error.message).not_to include('Known')
    97	      }
    98	    end
    99	
   100	    it 'includes provider-specific refresh guidance for unknown models' do
   101	      expect do
   102	        RubyLLM.models.find('nonexistent-model-12345', 'openai')
   103	      end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ModelNotFoundError) { |error|
   104	        expect(error.message).to include('Unknown model: "nonexistent-model-12345" for provider: "openai"')
   105	        expect(error.message).to include('RubyLLM.models.refresh!')
   106	        expect(error.message).to include('Model.refresh!')
   107	        expect(error.message).not_to include('Known')
   108	      }
   109	    end
   110	  end
   111	
   112	  describe '#find' do
   113	    it 'prioritizes exact matches over aliases' do
   114	      chat_model = RubyLLM.chat(model: 'gemini-2.0-flash')
   115	      expect(chat_model.model.id).to eq('gemini-2.0-flash')
   116	
   117	      chat_model = RubyLLM.chat(model: 'gemini-2.0-flash', provider: 'gemini')
   118	      expect(chat_model.model.id).to eq('gemini-2.0-flash')
   119	
   120	      # Only use alias when exact match isn't found
   121	      chat_model = RubyLLM.chat(model: 'claude-3-5-haiku')
   122	      expect(chat_model.model.id).to eq('claude-3-5-haiku-20241022')
   123	    end
   124	
   125	    it 'prefers bedrock region-resolved inference profile IDs over exact unprefixed IDs' do
   126	      unprefixed = RubyLLM::Model::Info.new(
   127	        id: 'meta.llama4-maverick-17b-instruct-v1:0',
   128	        name: 'Llama 4 Maverick',
   129	        provider: 'bedrock',
   130	        metadata: {}
   131	      )
   132	      prefixed = RubyLLM::Model::Info.new(
   133	        id: 'us.meta.llama4-maverick-17b-instruct-v1:0',
   134	        name: 'Llama 4 Maverick',
   135	        provider: 'bedrock',
   136	        metadata: { inference_types: ['INFERENCE_PROFILE'] }
   137	      )
   138	
   139	      models = described_class.new([unprefixed, prefixed])
   140	      allow(RubyLLM).to receive(:config).and_return(
   141	        instance_double(RubyLLM::Configuration, bedrock_region: 'us-west-2')
   142	      )
   143	
   144	      found = models.find('meta.llama4-maverick-17b-instruct-v1:0', 'bedrock')
   145	      expect(found.id).to eq('us.meta.llama4-maverick-17b-instruct-v1:0')
   146	    end
   147	  end
   148	
   149	  describe '#refresh!' do
   150	    before do
   151	      allow(described_class).to receive_messages(
   152	        fetch_provider_models: {
   153	          models: [],
   154	          fetched_providers: [],
   155	          configured_names: [],
   156	          failed: []
   157	        },
   158	        fetch_models_dev_models: { models: [], fetched: true }
   159	      )
   160	    end
   161	
   162	    it 'updates models and returns a chainable Models instance' do
   163	      # Refresh and chain immediately
   164	      chat_models = RubyLLM.models.refresh!.chat_models
   165	
   166	      # Verify we got results
   167	      expect(chat_models).to be_a(described_class)
   168	      expect(chat_models.all).to all(have_attributes(type: 'chat'))
   169	
   170	      # Verify we got models from at least OpenAI and Anthropic
   171	      providers = chat_models.map(&:provider).uniq
   172	      expect(providers).to include('openai', 'anthropic')
   173	    end
   174	
   175	    it 'works as a class method too' do
   176	      described_class.refresh!
   177	
   178	      # Verify singleton instance was updated
   179	      expect(RubyLLM.models.all.size).to be_positive
   180	    end
   181	  end
   182	
   183	  describe '.models_dev_model_to_info' do
   184	    let(:model_data) do

   186	        id: 'gpt-test-1',

   192	        tool_call: true,
   193	        structured_output: true,

   208	          output: 4096
   209	        }
   210	      }
   211	    end
   212	
   213	    it 'converts models.dev payload into a Model::Info-compatible hash' do
   214	      data = described_class.models_dev_model_to_info(model_data, 'openai', 'openai')
   215	
   216	      expect(data).to include(
   217	        id: 'gpt-test-1',
   218	        name: 'GPT Test 1',
   219	        provider: 'openai',
   220	        family: 'gpt-test',
   221	        context_window: 128_000,
   222	        max_output_tokens: 4096,
   223	        knowledge_cutoff: Date.parse('2024-01-01')

{
  "step_1_orient": {
    "project": "RubyLLM is a Ruby gem/framework that exposes one API over multiple AI providers; the gemspec describes support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Bedrock, DeepSeek, Mistral, Ollama, OpenRouter, Perplexity, GPUStack, and OpenAI-compatible APIs at ruby_llm.gemspec:11 and ruby_llm.gemspec:17.",
    "map": [
      "Public facade: lib/ruby_llm.rb:42 defines RubyLLM; public entry points include chat/embed/moderate/paint/transcribe/models at lib/ruby_llm.rb:58.",
      "Core chat object: lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:7; model/provider binding happens in Chat#initialize and Chat#with_model at lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:12 and lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:84.",
      "Provider base/registry: lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:10; provider registration, protocol selection, config validation, and shared error parsing live there.",
      "Concrete providers: lib/ruby_llm/providers/*.rb, for example OpenAI at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openai.rb:6 and Mistral at lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral.rb:6.",
      "Shared protocol/dialect layer: lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:7 plus protocol families under lib/ruby_llm/protocols/, especially OpenAI Chat Completions at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions.rb:6.",
      "Model registry/catalog: lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:8 and persisted catalog at lib/ruby_llm/models.json.",
      "HTTP and typed errors: lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb:11, lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:8, lib/ruby_llm/error.rb:6."

  "step_2_request_routing": [
    "1. User calls RubyLLM.chat; facade delegates to Chat.new at lib/ruby_llm.rb:58.",
    "2. Chat#initialize chooses model_id from argument or config.default_model, then calls with_model at lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:12 and lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:19.",
    "3. Chat#with_model calls Models.resolve and stores @model, @provider, and @connection at lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:84.",
    "4. Models.resolve uses an explicit provider via Provider.providers[provider.to_sym] or finds the model in the registry, then looks up Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym] at lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:132 and lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:153.",
    "5. Provider.providers is populated by RubyLLM::Provider.register calls in lib/ruby_llm.rb:104.",
    "6. Provider.new validates configuration and builds a Connection at lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:13.",
    "7. Chat#ask adds a user message and calls complete at lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:41.",
    "8. Chat#complete instruments and calls complete_once at lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:179.",
    "9. Chat#provider_completion delegates to @provider.complete at lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:295.",
    "10. Provider#complete resolves the protocol, instantiates it with provider/model, and calls protocol.complete at lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:54.",
    "11. Provider#resolve_protocol prefers explicit with_protocol, then configured <slug>_protocol, then provider protocol_for/default_protocol at lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:210.",
    "12. Protocol#complete renders payload and uses sync_response or stream_response at lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:20; sync chat POST happens at lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:109."
  ],
  "step_3_shared_chat_completions_dialect": {
    "shared_behavior": [
      "ChatCompletions includes shared Chat, Embeddings, Models, Moderation, Streaming, Tools, Images, Media, and Transcription modules at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions.rb:6.",
      "Temperature normalization is applied through maybe_normalize_temperature at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions.rb:17.",
      "Chat endpoint defaults to chat/completions at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/chat.rb:8.",
      "Payload rendering handles model, messages, stream, temperature, tools, tool_choice, parallel_tool_calls, JSON schema response_format, reasoning_effort, and stream_options at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/chat.rb:15.",
      "Response parsing builds Message objects, tool calls, citations, thinking fields, token usage, and raw response at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/chat.rb:62.",
      "Message formatting maps system to developer unless openai_use_system_role is true at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/chat.rb:187 and lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/chat.rb:213.",
      "Streaming chunks are parsed into Chunk objects at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/streaming.rb:16.",
      "Model listing expects GET models with response.body['data'] at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/models.rb:10."
    ],
    "customizations": [
      "OpenAI registers both Responses and ChatCompletions; Responses is default, but audio/realtime/search-preview route to ChatCompletions at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openai.rb:7 and lib/ruby_llm/providers/openai.rb:15.",
      "DeepSeek subclasses ChatCompletions and includes DeepSeek::Chat at lib/ruby_llm/providers/deepseek.rb:8; it forces roles to strings and disables document/image/audio attachments in media formatting at lib/ruby_llm/providers/deepseek/chat.rb:10.",
      "Mistral subclasses ChatCompletions and includes custom Chat, Embeddings, Media, and Models at lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral.rb:8. Chat removes stream_options, customizes thinking payloads, maps required tool_choice to any, and normalizes one-tool required choice at lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/chat.rb:26. Media uses image_url/document_url shapes at lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/media.rb:10. Embeddings omit dimensions and parse Mistral usage at lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/embeddings.rb:10. Models enrich names/family/modalities/capabilities/pricing at lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/models.rb:16.",
      "Ollama subclasses ChatCompletions and includes custom Chat, Media, and Models at lib/ruby_llm/providers/ollama.rb:8. It formats roles as strings at lib/ruby_llm/providers/ollama/chat.rb:14, supports only image/text attachments at lib/ruby_llm/providers/ollama/media.rb:10, and parses local model metadata at lib/ruby_llm/providers/ollama/models.rb:12.",
      "GPUStack subclasses Ollama::ChatCompletions and swaps only model parsing via GPUStack::Models at lib/ruby_llm/providers/gpustack.rb:8; it maps GPUStack item metadata to capabilities/modalities at lib/ruby_llm/providers/gpustack/models.rb:16.",
      "OpenRouter subclasses ChatCompletions and includes custom Chat, Images, Models, and Streaming at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter.rb:8. Chat removes reasoning_effort, strips strict from JSON schema, and emits OpenRouter reasoning payloads at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter/chat.rb:11. Images use chat/completions for image generation at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter/images.rb:12. Streaming customizes chunk thinking parsing at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter/streaming.rb:10. Models parse OpenRouter metadata and supported_parameters at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter/models.rb:14.",
      "Perplexity subclasses ChatCompletions and includes custom Chat and Models at lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity.rb:8. Chat formats roles and media at lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity/chat.rb:10. Media supports image, pdf/document, and text with file_url for documents at lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity/media.rb:12. Models are hard-coded instead of fetched at lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity/models.rb:8 and lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity/models.rb:16.",
      "xAI subclasses ChatCompletions and includes custom Chat and Models at lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai.rb:8. Chat disables document/audio and keeps image support at lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai/chat.rb:13. Models infer image/video/text modalities and capabilities from model IDs at lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai/models.rb:10.",
      "Azure uses Azure::ChatCompletions, a ChatCompletions subclass with endpoint rewriting for resource, deployment, openai/v1, or full chat endpoint bases at lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure/chat_completions.rb:8 and lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure/chat_completions.rb:17. Azure Chat overrides completion_url/roles/media at lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure/chat.rb:8; embeddings route through azure_endpoint at lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure/embeddings.rb:10; media supports image/audio/text only at lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure/media.rb:10; models_url uses azure_endpoint(:models) at lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure/models.rb:8."
    ]
  },
  "step_4_settings_declaration_and_validation": [
    "Providers declare configuration_options and configuration_requirements on the provider class; examples: OpenAI at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openai.rb:32, DeepSeek at lib/ruby_llm/providers/deepseek.rb:29, Mistral at lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral.rb:32, OpenRouter at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter.rb:44, Perplexity at lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity.rb:47, xAI at lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai.rb:27, Azure at lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure.rb:22.",
    "Configuration supports dynamic options via Configuration.option and register_provider_options at lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb:9 and lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb:25.",
    "Provider.register stores the provider in Provider.providers and registers provider_class.configuration_options plus <slug>_protocol at lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:174.",
    "All providers are registered in lib/ruby_llm.rb:104.",
    "Default configured? checks all configuration_requirements at lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:159.",
    "Provider#initialize calls ensure_configured! before creating the connection at lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:13.",
    "Provider#ensure_configured! computes missing requirements and raises ConfigurationError with a config snippet at lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:237.",
    "Azure customizes configured? to require azure_api_base plus either azure_api_key or azure_ai_auth_token at lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure.rb:30 and customizes ensure_configured! at lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure.rb:40.",
    "Specs enforce requirements are a subset of options and provider options are exposed through Configuration at spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:192."

  "step_5_http_failure_to_typed_error": [
    "Connection installs Faraday JSON request/response middleware and then llm_errors with the provider at lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb:112.",
    "The llm_errors middleware is registered at lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:90.",
    "ErrorMiddleware#call receives the raw Faraday response on_complete and calls parse_error at lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:14.",
    "ErrorMiddleware.parse_error first asks provider.parse_error(response) for a message at lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:41.",
    "Default Provider#parse_error parses Hash/Array/string response bodies and checks error.message, message, and detail at lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:104.",
    "Status mapping: 200..399 returns message; 400 maps to ContextLengthExceededError if patterns match else BadRequestError; 401 UnauthorizedError; 402 PaymentRequiredError; 403 ForbiddenError; 429 RateLimitError or ContextLengthExceededError; 500 ServerError; 502..504 ServiceUnavailableError; 529 OverloadedError; else generic Error at lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:44.",
    "Typed error classes are defined in lib/ruby_llm/error.rb:42 through lib/ruby_llm/error.rb:104.",
    "Context-length and rate-limit regex classification lives at lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:21 and lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:34.",
    "OpenRouter customizes parse_error to unwrap nested metadata.raw messages at lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter.rb:27 and lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter.rb:55.",
    "Perplexity customizes parse_error for HTML title auth errors, then falls back to super at lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity.rb:26.",
    "Bedrock customizes parse_error to read AWS error shapes at lib/ruby_llm/providers/bedrock.rb:24.",
    "Streaming failures use Streaming#raise_stream_error, build a response-like object, and call ErrorMiddleware.parse_error at lib/ruby_llm/streaming.rb:125.",
    "Generic streaming parse_streaming_error defaults to 500/message at lib/ruby_llm/streaming.rb:117; ChatCompletions maps server_error to 500, rate_limit_exceeded/insufficient_quota to 429, else 400 at lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/streaming.rb:47."
  ],
